It returns a list of validation errors\n107\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n108\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n109\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n110\t\n111\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n112\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n113\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n114\t\n115\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n116\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n117\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n118\t                {\n119\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n120\t                }\n121\t                scope_stack.reset();\n122\t                continue;\n123\t            }\n124\t\n125\t            // Skip YAML directives\n126\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n127\t                continue;\n128\t            }\n129\t\n130\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n131\t\n132\t            // Handle blank lines and comments\n133\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n134\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n135\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n136\t                // Track blank lines with different indentation for debugging, but don't\n137\t                // change scope. Scope transitions only happen on content lines.\n138\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n139\t                if indent != scope_stack.current_indent() && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n140\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Blank line with indent change detected: line={}, indent={}, current_indent={}, skipping (will handle on next content line)\",\n141\t                        line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.current_indent());\n142\t                }\n143\t                continue;\n144\t            }\n145\t\n146\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n147\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n148\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n149\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n150\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n151\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n152\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n153\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n154\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n155\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n156\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n157\t                                    dup_err.message()\n158\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n159\t                            }\n160\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n161\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n162\t                            {\n163\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={},                            indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n168\t                                line_num_1index,\n169\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n170\t                            );\n171\t                        } else {\n172\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n173\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n174\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n175\t                            {\n176\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n177\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, dup_err.message()\n183\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n184\t                            }\n185\t                        }\n186\t                    } else {\n187\t        {\n193\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n194\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n195\t                        }\n196\t                    }\n197\t                }\n198\t                Ordering::Less => {\n199\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n200\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n201\t                    {\n202\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n203\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n204\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n205\t                    }\n206\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n207\t\n208\t                    // After                       ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n234\t                            }\n235\t                        }\n236\t                    }\n237\t                    // Edge case: indent             if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n242\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n243\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent                         line_num_1index,\n266\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n267\t                            );\n268\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n269\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n270\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n271\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n272\t                                    dup_err.message()\n273\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n274\t                            }\n275\t                        }\n276\t                    }\n277\t                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n278\t                }\n279\t            }\n280\t\n281\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n282\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n283\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n284\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n285\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n286\t                    {\n287\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n288\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n289\t                    }\n290\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n291\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n292\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n293\t                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n294\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n295\t                            dup_err.message()\n296\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n297\t                    }\n298\t                } else {\n299\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n300\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n301\t                    {\n302\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n303\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n304\t                    }\n305\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n306\t                }\n307\t            }\n308\t }\n318\t}\n319\t\n320\timpl Parser for BasicParser {\n321\t    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n322\t        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing                  log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n476\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n477\t                            }\n478\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n479\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n480\t                                line_num_1index,\n481\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n482\t                            );\n483\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n484\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n485\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n486\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n487\t                                    dup_err.message()\n488\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n489\t                            }\n490\t                        }\n491\t                    }\n492\t                }\n493\t            }\n494\t\n495\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n496\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n497\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n498\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n499\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n500\t                    {\n501\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n502\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n503\t                    }\n504\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n505\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n506\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n507\t                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n508\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n509\t                            dup_err.message()\n510\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n511\t                    }\n512\t                } else {\n513\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n514\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n515\t                    {\n516\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n517\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n518\t                    }\n519\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n520\t                }\n521\t            }\n522\t        }\n523\t\n524\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n525\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n526\t            Ok(value) => {\n527\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n528\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n529\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n530\t            }\n531\t            Err(err) => {\n532\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n533\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n534\t                    err.to_string()\n535\t                } else {\n536\t                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n537\t                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n538\t                };\n539\t                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n540\t            }\n541\t        }\n542\t    }\n543\t\n544\t    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n545\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n546\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n547\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n548\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n549\t        }\n550\t    }\n551\t\n552\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n553\t        // Read file content\n554\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n555\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n556\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n557\t        }\n558\t    }\n559\t\n560\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n561\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n562\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n563\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n564\t        } else {\n565\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n566\t        };\n567\t\n568\t        // Run syntax validation\n569\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n570\t\n571\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n572\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n573\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n574\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n575\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n576\t                result.valid = false;\n577\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n578\t            }\n579\t        }\n580\t\n581\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n582\t        if result.is_valid() {\n583\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n584\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n585\t\n586\t            // Merge errors from detector\n587\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n588\t                result.valid = false;\n589\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n590\t            }\n591\t        }\n592\t\n593\t        result\n594\t    }\n595\t\n596\t    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n597\t        // Read file content\n598\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n599\t            Ok(content) => content,\n600\t            Err(err) => {\n601\t                return ValidationResult {\n602\t                    valid: false,\n603\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n604\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n605\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n606\t                    )],\n607\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n608\t                };\n609\t            }\n610\t        };\n611\t\n612\t        // Validate the content\n613\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n614\t    }\n615\t\n616\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n617\t        &self.config\n618\t    }\n619\t\n620\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n621\t    where\n622\t        Self: Sized,\n623\t    {\n624\t        Self {\n625\t            config,\n626\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n627\t        }\n628\t    }\n629\t}\n630\t\n631\t// ============================================================================\n632\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n633\t// ============================================================================\n634\t\n635\t#[cfg(test)]\n636\tmod integration_tests {\n637\t    use super::*;\n638\t\n639\t    /// Test parse_str with Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the Parser trait and provides functionality for\n//! parsing YAML content from various sources.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::{\n    types::{ParseResult, ValidationResult, ValidationError},\n    error::ParseError,\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n    scope::ScopeStack,\n    line_parser::{calculate_indentation},\n    scope::extract_key_context,\n};\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n    ///\n    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Track blank lines with different indentation for debugging, but don't\n                // change scope. Scope transitions only happen on content lines.\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                if indent != scope_stack.current_indent() && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Blank line with indent change detected: line={}, indent={}, current_indent={}, skipping (will handle on next content line)\",\n                        line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.current_indent());\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        duplicate_errors\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for BasicParser {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Parser for BasicParser {\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Track blank lines with different indentation for debugging, but don't\n                // change scope. Scope transitions only happen on content lines.\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                if indent != scope_stack.current_indent() && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Blank line with indent change detected: line={}, indent={}, current_indent={}, skipping (will handle on next content line)\",\n                        line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.current_indent());\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n            Ok(value) => {\n                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n                ParseResult::success(value)\n            }\n            Err(err) => {\n                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n                    err.to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n                };\n                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Read file content\n        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n        } else {\n            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n        };\n\n        // Run syntax validation\n        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n\n        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n        if result.is_valid() {\n            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n\n            // Merge errors from detector\n            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Read file content\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => content,\n            Err(err) => {\n                return ValidationResult {\n                    valid: false,\n                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n                        path.display().to_string(),\n                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n                    )],\n                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n                };\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Validate the content\n        self.validate_str(&content)\n    }\n\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n        &self.config\n    }\n\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized,\n    {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod integration_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Test parse_str with nested YAML structures\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse valid nested YAML successfully\");\n\n        // Verify we can access the parsed structure\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with deeply nested YAML\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_deeply_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\napplication:\n  server:\n    config:\n      timeouts:\n        connect: 30\n        read: 60\n    logging:\n      level: info\n  database:\n    connection:\n      pool:\n        max: 10\n        min: 2\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse deeply nested YAML successfully\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that same key in different scopes passes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_same_key_different_scopes_passes() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        // This YAML has 'host' and 'port' in different scopes\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n  cache:\n    host: redis.example.com\n    port: 6379\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Same key in different scopes should be allowed\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let services = &value[\"services\"];\n        assert_eq!(services[\"web\"][\"host\"], \"localhost\");\n        assert_eq!(services[\"database\"][\"host\"], \"db.example.com\");\n        assert_eq!(services[\"cache\"][\"host\"], \"redis.example.com\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that duplicate key in same scope is detected by strict parser\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_same_scope_detected() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict(); // Strict parser doesn't allow duplicates\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nconfig:\n  server:\n    host: localhost\n    host: duplicate-value\n    port: 8080\n\"#;\n\n        // The parse_str itself may succeed (serde_yaml's behavior), but validation should detect the duplicate\n        let validation_result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!validation_result.is_valid(), \"Validation should detect duplicate keys\");\n\n        // Check that error message mentions duplicate key\n        let error_messages: Vec<String> = validation_result.errors\n            .iter()\n            .map(|e| e.message.clone())\n            .collect();\n        assert!(error_messages.iter().any(|msg| msg.contains(\"duplicate\") || msg.contains(\"host\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with sequence items having same keys\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_sequence_items_same_keys() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  - name: item1\n    value: 100\n  - name: item2\n    value: 200\n  - name: item3\n    value: 300\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Sequence items with same keys should be allowed\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let items = value[\"items\"].as_sequence().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(items.len(), 3);\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with mixed mapping and sequence scopes\n    #[test]\n    fn host: db1.example.com\n    port: 5432\n  replica:\n    host: db2.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\nlogging:\n  level: info\n  outputs:\n    - type: stdout\n      format: json\n    - type: file\n correctly\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that parent key followed by nested content creates proper scope\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parent_key_creates_scope() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nparent:\n  child1: value1\n  child2: value2\n  nested:\n    deep: value3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Parent key with nested content should create proper scope\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(value[\"parent\"][\"child1\"], \"value1\");\n        assert_eq!(value[\"parent\"][\"nested\"][\"deep\"], \"value3\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test sequence scope isolation\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_isolation() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  - id: 1\n    name: First\n    config:\n      enabled: true\n  - id: 2\n    name: Second\n    config:\n      enabled: false\n  - id: 3\n    name: Third\n    config:\n      enabled: true\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Sequence items should have isolated scopes\");\n\n scopes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_multiple_scopes_with_duplicates() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nscope1:\n  key: value1\n  key: parse_str with various indentation patterns\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_various_indentation_patterns() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nroot:\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Various indentation patterns should be handled correctly\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that empty document parses successfully\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_empty_document() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = \"\";\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        // Empty document should parse as null\n        assert!(result.is_success());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that document with only comments parses successfully\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_only_comments() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\n# Comment 1\n# Comment 2\n# Comment 3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Document with only comments should parse successfully\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test validation error message quality\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validation_error_message_quality() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nconfig:\n  server:\n    host: localhost\n    host: duplicate\n    port: 8080\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!result.is_valid());\n\n        // Check that error messages are useful\n        for error in &result.errors {\n            assert!(!error.message.is_empty(), \"Error message should not be empty\");\n            if error.line.is_some() {\n                assert!(error.message.contains(\"Line\"), \"Error should mention line number\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test blank line with decreased indent (scope should exit properly)\n    #[test]\n    fn test_blank_line_with_decreased_indent() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nroot:\n  nested:\n    key1: value1\n\nkey2: value2\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Blank line with decreased indent should parse value3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Blank line at same indent should parse successfully\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let root = &value[\"root\"];\n        assert_eq!(root[\"key1\"], \"value1\");\n        assert_eq!(root[\"key2\"], \"value2\");\n        assert_eq!(root[\"key3\"], \"value3\");\n    }\n\n    /// level2:\n    key1: value1\n\n\n  key2: value2\n\nlevel3: value3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Multiple blank lines at various indents value1\n  # Comment at indent 2\n  key2: value2\n# Comment at indent 0\nkey3: value3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Comments at different indents should not affect parsing\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let root = &value[\"root\"];\n        assert_eq!(root[\"key1\"], \"value1\");\n        assert_eq!(root[\"key2\"], \"value2\");\n        assert_eq!(value[\"key3\"], \"value3\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test blank line followed by key at same indent\n    #[test]\n    fn test_blank_line_key_same_indent() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Blank line followed by key at same indent should parse successfully\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let services = &value[\"services\"];\n        assert!(services.get(\"web\").is_some());\n        assert!(services.get(\"database\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test indent transition without key (just blank line)\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indent_transition_blank_line_only() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nlevel1:\n  level2:\n    key1: value1\n\n\nkey3: value3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Indent transition via blank lines should parse successfully\");\n\n        let value = {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_80198045f7804b5eab36f120","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! YAML Line Parser Module\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module provides the core data structures for representing parsed YAML lines.\n4\t//! It defines types that hold line-by-line parsing results, including line type,\n5\t//! indentation level, and raw content.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! ## Architecture\n8\t//!\n9\t//! The line parser module provides the foundational types for line-based YAML analysis:\n10\t//!\n11\t//! - [`LineType`] - Enumeration of all possible YAML line types\n12\t//! - [`YamlLine`] - Struct representing a single parsed YAML line\n13\t//! - [`LineContent`] - Structured content representation for different line types\n14\t//!\n15\t//! ## Usage\n16\t//!\n17\t//! ```ignore\n18\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::{YamlLine, LineType};\n19\t//!\n20\t//! let line = YamlLine::new(1, \"key: value\", 0, LineType::MappingKey);\n21\t//! assert_eq!(line.line_number(), 1);\n22\t//! assert_eq!(line.raw_content(), \"key: value\");\n23\t//! ```\n24\t//!\n25\t//! ## Line Type Categories\n26\t//!\n27\t//! - **Structural**: Blank lines, document markers\n28\t//! - **Comments**: Full and inline comments\n29\t//! - **Content**: Mapping keys, values, sequence items\n30\t//! - **Special**: Anchors, aliases, tags, directives\n31\t\n32\tuse std::fmt;\n33\t\n34\t/// Classification of YAML line types\n35\t///\n36\t/// This enum provides structured categorization of different line types\n37\t/// that can occur in YAML files. Each variant represents a specific semantic\n38\t/// category recognized by the YAML specification.\n39\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n40\tpub enum LineType {\n41\t    /// Empty line (only whitespace or no characters)\n42\t    Blank,\n43\t\n44\t    /// Comment line (starts with #)\n45\t    Comment,\n46\t\n47\t    /// Document start marker (---)\n48\t    DocumentStart,\n49\t\n50\t    /// Document end marker (...)\n51\t    DocumentEnd,\n52\t\n53\t    /// Mapping key line (e.g., \"key:\" or \"key: value\")\n54\t    MappingKey,\n55\t\n56\t    /// Mapping value line (value after colon on same line)\n57\t    MappingValue,\n58\t\n59\t    /// Sequence item line (starts with \"- \")\n60\t    SequenceItem,\n61\t\n62\t    /// Flow style mapping (starts with {)\n63\t    FlowMapping,\n64\t\n65\t    /// Flow style sequence (starts with [)\n66\t    FlowSequence,\n67\t\n68\t    /// Anchor definition (starts with &)\n69\t    Anchor,\n70\t\n71\t    /// Alias reference (starts with *)\n72\t    Alias,\n73\t\n74\t    /// Tag directive (starts with !)\n75\t    Tag,\n76\t\n77\t    /// YAML directive (starts with %)\n78\t    Directive,\n79\t\n80\t    /// Literal block scalar (starts with |)\n81\t    LiteralBlockScalar,\n82\t\n83\t    /// Folded block scalar (starts with >)\n84\t    FoldedBlockScalar,\n85\t\n86\t    /// Unrecognized content (fallback for unknown patterns)\n87\t    Unknown,\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\timpl LineType {\n91\t    /// Get a human-readable description of this line type\n92\t    pub fn description(&self) -> &'static str {\n93\t        match self {\n94\t            Self::Blank => \"blank line\",\n95\t            Self::Comment => \"comment line\",\n96\t            Self::DocumentStart => \"document start marker\",\n97\t            Self::DocumentEnd => \"document end marker\",\n98\t            Self::MappingKey => \"mapping key\",\n99\t            Self::MappingValue => \"mapping value\",\n100\t            Self::SequenceItem => \"sequence item\",\n101\t            Self::FlowMapping => \"flow style mapping\",\n102\t            Self::FlowSequence => \"flow style sequence\",\n103\t            Self::Anchor => \"anchor definition\",\n104\t            Self::Alias => \"alias reference\",\n105\t            Self::Tag => \"tag directive\",\n106\t            Self::Directive => \"YAML directive\",\n107\t            Self::LiteralBlockScalar => \"literal block scalar\",\n108\t            Self::FoldedBlockScalar => \"folded block scalar\",\n109\t            Self::Unknown => \"unknown content\",\n110\t        }\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    /// Check if this line type is a structural element\n114\t    ///\n115\t    /// Structural elements are blank lines, document markers, and comments\n116\t    /// that don't contribute to the actual data content.\n117\t    pub fn is_structural(&self) -> bool {\n118\t        matches!(\n119\t            self,\n120\t            Self::Blank | Self::DocumentStart | Self::DocumentEnd | Self::Comment\n121\t        )\n122\t    }\n123\t\n124\t    /// Check if this line type is meaningful content\n125\t    ///\n126\t    /// Meaningful content includes keys, values, and data items.\n127\t    pub fn is_content(&self) -> bool {\n128\t        matches!(\n129\t            self,\n130\t            Self::MappingKey |\n131\t            Self::MappingValue |\n132\t            Self::SequenceItem |\n133\t            Self::FlowMapping |\n134\t            Self::FlowSequence |\n135\t            Self::LiteralBlockScalar |\n136\t            Self::FoldedBlockScalar\n137\t        )\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Check if this line type is a YAML directive or special marker\n141\t    pub fn is_directive(&self) -> bool {\n142\t        matches!(self, Self::Directive | Self::Tag | Self::Anchor | Self::Alias)\n143\t    }\n144\t\n145\t    /// Get all line type variants\n146\t    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {\n147\t        &[\n148\t            Self::Blank,\n149\t            Self::Comment,\n150\t            Self::DocumentStart,\n151\t            Self::DocumentEnd,\n152\t            Self::MappingKey,\n153\t            Self::MappingValue,\n154\t            Self::SequenceItem,\n155\t            Self::FlowMapping,\n156\t            Self::FlowSequence,\n157\t            Self::Anchor,\n158\t            Self::Alias,\n159\t            Self::Tag,\n160\t fmt::Display for LineType {\n169\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n170\t        write!(f, \"{}\", self.description())\n171\t    }\n172\t}\n173\t\n174\t/// Structured content representation for different line types\n175\t///\n176\t/// This enum provides typed access to the semantic content of a parsed line,\n177\t/// allowing type-safe extraction of line-specific data.\n178\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n179\tpub enum LineContent {\n180\t    /// No content (blank lines, document markers)\n181\t    Empty,\n182\t\n183\t    /// Comment content (text after #)\n184\t    Comment(String),\n185\t\n186\t    /// Mapping key content (key name and optional value)\n187\t    MappingKey {\n188\t        /// The key name\n189\t        key: String,\n190\t        /// The value, if item2]\")\n203\t        content: String,\n204\t    },\n205\t\n206\t    /// Anchor definition\n207\t    Anchor {\n208\t        /// The anchor name\n209\t        name: String,\n210\t    },\n211\t\n212\t    /// Alias reference\n213\t    Alias {\n214\t        /// The alias name\n215\t        name: String,\n216\t    },\n217\t\n218\t    /// Tag directive\n219\t    Tag {\n220\t        /// The tag The block type indicator (|, >, |-, >-, etc.)\n235\t        indicator: String,\n236\t        /// The optional header comment or content\n237\t        header: Option<String>,\n238\t    },\n239\t\n240\t    /// Unknown content\n241\t    Unknown(String),\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\timpl LineContent {\n245\t    /// Check if this content is empty\n246\t    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n247\t        matches!(self, Self::Empty)\n248\t    }\n249\t\n250\t    /// Get the content as a string slice, if applicable\n251\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> Option<&str> {\n252\t        match self {\n253\t            Self::Comment(s) => Some(s),\n254\t            Self::MappingKey { key, .. } => Some(key),\n255\t            Self::SequenceItem { value } => Some(value),\n256\t            Self::FlowContent { content } => Some(content),\n257\t            Self::Anchor { name } => Some(name),\n258\t            Self::Alias { name } => Some(name),\n259\t            Self::Tag { tag } => Some(tag),\n260\t            Self::Directive { parameters, .. } => Some(parameters),\n261\t            Self::BlockScalar { header, .. } => header.as_deref(),\n262\t            Self::Empty | Self::Unknown(_) => None,\n263\t        }\n264\t    }\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\timpl fmt::Display for LineContent {\n268\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n269\t        match self {\n270\t            Self::Empty => write!(f, \"<empty>\"),\n271\t            Self::Comment(s) => write!(f, \"# {}\", s),\n272\t            Self::MappingKey { key, value } => {\n273\t                if let Some(v) = value {\n274\t                    write!(f, \"{}: {}\", key, v)\n275\t                } else {\n276\t                    write!(f, \"{}:\", key)\n277\t                }\n278\t            }\n279\t            Self::SequenceItem { value } => write!(f, \"- {}\", value),\n280\t            Self::FlowContent { content } => write!(f, \"{}\", content),\n281\t            Self::Anchor { name } => write!(f, \"&{}\", name),\n282\t            Self::Alias { name } => write!(f, \"*{}\", name),\n283\t            Self::Tag { tag } => write!(f, \"!{}\", tag),\n284\t            Self::Directive { directive_type, parameters } => {\n285\t                write!(f, \"%{} {}\", directive_type, parameters)\n286\t            }\n287\t            Self::BlockScalar { indicator, header } => {\n288\t                write!(f, \"{}\", indicator)?;\n289\t                if let Some(h) = header {\n290\t                    write!(f, \" {}\", h)\n291\t                } else {\n292\t                    Ok(())\n293\t                }\n294\t            }\n295\t            Self::Unknown(s) => write!(f, \"<unknown: {}>\", s),\n296\t        }\n297\t    }\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t/// Represents a single parsed YAML
