   let mut leading_tabs = 0;\n564\t\n565\t        for ch in line.chars() {\n566\t            match ch {\n567\t                ' ' => leading_spaces += 1,\n568\t                '\\t' => leading_tabs += 1,\n569\t                _ => break, // Stop at first non-whitespace character\n570\t            }\n571\t        }\n572\t\n573\t        Self {\n574\t            leading_spaces,\n575\t            leading_tabs,\n576\t            total_level: leading_spaces + leading_tabs,\n577\t        }\n578\t    }\n579\t\n580\t    /// Check if the line has mixed indentation (both spaces and tabs)\n581\t    ///\n582\t    /// # Returns\n583\t    /// `true` if the line contains both spaces and tabs in leading whitespace\n584\t    pub fn is_mixed(&self) -> bool {\n585\t        self.leading_spaces > 0 && self.leading_tabs > 0\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    /// Check if the line uses only tab indentation\n589\t    ///\n590\t    /// # Returns\n591\t    /// `true` if the line uses only tabs for indentation\n592\t    pub fn is_tabs_only(&self) -> bool {\n593\t        self.leading_tabs > 0 && self.leading_spaces == 0\n594\t    }\n595\t\n596\t    /// Check if the line uses only space indentation\n597\t    ///\n598\t    /// # Returns\n599\t    /// `true` if the line uses only spaces for indentation\n600\t    pub fn is_spaces_only(&self) -> bool {\n601\t        self.leading_spaces > 0 && self.leading_tabs == 0\n602\t    }\n603\t\n604\t    /// Check if the line has no indentation\n605\t    ///\n606\t    /// # Returns\n607\t    /// `true` if the line has no leading whitespace\n608\t    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n609\t        self.total_level == 0\n610\t    }\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\timpl fmt::Display for IndentationInfo {\n614\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n615\t        if self.is_mixed() {\n616\t            write!(\n617\t                f,\n618\t                \"{} spaces + {} tabs (MIXED)\",\n619\t                self.leading_spaces, self.leading_tabs\n620\t            )\n621\t        } else if self.is_tabs_only() {\n622\t            write!(f, \"{} tabs\", self.leading_tabs)\n623\t        } else if self.is_spaces_only() {\n624\t            write!(f, \"{} spaces\", self.leading_spaces)\n625\t        } else {\n626\t            write!(f, \"no indentation\")\n627\t        }\n628\t    }\n629\t}\n630\t\n631\t/// Classify a YAML line into its type\n632\t///\n633\t/// This function analyzes a line and determines its type based on YAML\n634\t/// specification rules and common patterns.\n635\t///\n636\t/// # Arguments\n637\t///\n638\t/// * `line` - The line to classify\n639\t///\n640\t/// # Returns\n641\t/// The classified `LineType` for the line\n642\t///\n643\t/// # Examples\n644\t///\n645\t/// ```\n646\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::{classify_line_type, LineType};\n647\t///\n648\t/// assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\"), LineType::Blank);\n649\t/// assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  \"), LineType::Blank);\n650\t/// assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"# comment\"), LineType::Comment);\n651\t/// assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"---\"), LineType::DocumentStart);\n652\t/// assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"key: value\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n653\t/// ```\n654\tpub fn classify_line_type(line: &str) -> LineType {\n655\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n656\t\n657\t    // Empty lines (including whitespace-only lines)\n658\t    if trimmed.is_empty() {\n659\t        return LineType::Blank;\n660\t    }\n661\t\n662\t    // Comment lines\n663\t    if trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n664\t        return LineType::Comment;\n665\t    }\n666\t\n667\t    // Document markers\n668\t    if trimmed == \"---\" {\n669\t        return LineType::DocumentStart;\n670\t    }\n671\t    if trimmed == \"...\" {\n672\t        return LineType::DocumentEnd;\n673\t    }\n674\t\n675\t    // YAML directives (start with %)\n676\t    if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n677\t        return LineType::Directive;\n678\t    }\n679\t\n680\t    // Tags (start with !)\n681\t    if trimmed.starts_with('!') {\n682\t        return LineType::Tag;\n683\t    }\n684\t\n685\t    // Anchors (start with &)\n686\t    if trimmed.starts_with('&') {\n687\t        return LineType::Anchor;\n688\t    }\n689\t\n690\t    // Aliases (start with *)\n691\t    if trimmed.starts_with('*') {\n692\t        return LineType::Alias;\n693\t    }\n694\t\n695\t    // Literal block scalar (|)\n696\t    if trimmed.starts_with('|') {\n697\t        return LineType::LiteralBlockScalar;\n698\t    }\n699\t\n700\t    // Folded block scalar (>)\n701\t    if trimmed.starts_with('>') {\n702\t        return LineType::FoldedBlockScalar;\n703\t    }\n704\t\n705\t    // Sequence items (start with -)\n706\t    // Note: YAML spec requires \"- \" but we're lenient for lone \"-\" as well\n707\t    if trimmed.starts_with(\"-\") {\n708\t        return LineType::SequenceItem;\n709\t    }\n710\t\n711\t    // Flow style mapping (starts with {)\n712\t    if trimmed.contains('{') && !trimmed.contains(':') {\n713\t        return LineType::FlowMapping;\n714\t    }\n715\t\n716\t    // Flow style sequence (starts with [)\n717\t    if trimmed.contains('[') {\n718\t        return LineType::FlowSequence;\n719\t    }\n720\t\n721\t    // Default to mapping key for most content lines\n722\t    // This handles \"key: value\" patterns\n723\t    if trimmed.contains(':') {\n724\t        return LineType::MappingKey;\n725\t    }\n726\t\n727\t    // Default to Unknown for lines without special patterns\n728\t    // This handles literal content lines that may contain ! or other characters\n729\t    LineType::Unknown\n730\t}\n731\t\n732\t/// Calculate indentation level from a line\n733\t///\n734\t/// This function counts the leading whitespace characters in a line.\n735\t/// It handles both spaces and tabs, counting each character individually.\n736\t///\n737\t/// **Note on tab vs space indentation**: YAML specification allows both\n738\t/// space and tab indentation, but they should never be mixed in the same\n739\t/// document. This function counts tabs and spaces equally (1 tab = 1 level)\n740\t/// for basic indentation tracking. For proper validation, use the\n741\t/// `IndentationInfo::from_line()` function which provides separate counts\n742\t/// and can detect mixed indentation.\n743\t///\n744\t/// # Arguments\n745\t///\n746\t/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n747\t///\n748\t/// # Returns\n749\t/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n750\t///\n751\t/// # Examples\n752\t///\n753\t/// ```\n754\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::calculate_indentation;\n755\t///\n756\t/// assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"key: value\"), 0);\n757\t/// assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n758\t/// assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\tkey: value\"), 1);\n759\t/// assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"    key: value\"), 4);\n760\t/// ```\n761\tpub fn calculate_indentation(line: &str) -> usize {\n762\t    IndentationInfo::from_line(line).total_level\n763\t}\n764\t\n765\t/// Information about a detected mapping key\n766\t///\n767\t/// This struct provides detailed information about a mapping key detected\n768\t/// in a YAML line, including the key identifier and metadata.\n769\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n770\tpub struct MappingKeyInfo {\n771\t    /// The key identifier (text before the colon, trimmed)\n772\t    pub key: String,\n773\t\n774\t    /// The value part, if present on the same line\n775\t    pub value: Option<String>,\n776\t\n777\t    /// Whether the key has a value on the same line\n778\t    pub has_inline_value: bool,\n779\t\n780\t    /// Whether the line appears to be a parent key (no value on same line)\n781\t    pub is_parent_key: bool,\n782\t}\n783\t\n784\timpl MappingKeyInfo {\n785\t    /// Create a new mapping key info\n786\t    ///\n787\t    /// # Arguments\n788\t    ///\n789\t    /// * `key` - The key identifier\n790\t    /// * `value` - Optional value from the same line\n791\t    ///\n792\t    /// # Returns\n793\t    /// A new `MappingKeyInfo` instance\n794\t    pub fn new(key: String, value: Option<String>) -> Self {\n795\t        let has_inline_value = value.is_some();\n796\t        let is_parent_key = !has_inline_value;\n797\t\n798\t        Self {\n799\t            key,\n800\t            value,\n801\t            has_inline_value,\n802\t            is_parent_key,\n803\t        }\n804\t    }\n805\t\n806\t    /// Check if this is a valid key (non-empty and follows YAML key rules)\n807\t    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {\n808\t        !self.key.is_empty() && self.key.chars().next().map_or(false, |c| {\n809\t            c.is_alphanumeric() || c == '_' || c == '.' || c == '-'\n810\t        })\n811\t    }\n812\t}\n813\t\n814\timpl fmt::Display for MappingKeyInfo {\n815\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n816\t        if let Some(v) = &self.value {\n817\t            write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.key, v)\n818\t        } else {\n819\t            write!(f, \"{}:\", self.key)\n820\t        }\n821\t    }\n822\t}\n823\t\n824\t/// Check if a line is a YAML comment line\n825\t///\n826\t/// This function determines if a line is a full-line comment (starts with #)\n827\t/// or contains inline content. It handles leading whitespace properly.\n828\t///\n829\t/// # Arguments\n830\t///\n831\t/// * `line` - The line to check\n832\t///\n833\t/// # Returns\n834\t///\n835\t/// `true` if the line is a comment line, `false` otherwise\n836\t///\n837\t/// # Examples\n838\t///\n839\t/// ```\n840\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::is_comment_line;\n841\t///\n842\t/// assert!(is_comment_line(\"# This is a comment\"));\n843\t/// assert!(is_comment_line(\"  # indented comment\"));\n844\t/// assert!(!is_comment_line(\"key: value # not a comment line\"));\n845\t/// assert!(!is_comment_line(\"key: value\"));\n846\t/// ```\n847\tpub fn is_comment_line(line: &str) -> bool {\n848\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n849\t    trimmed.starts_with('#')\n850\t}\n851\t\n852\t/// Check if a line contains unquoted brackets or braces\n853\t///\n854\t/// This helper function checks if `{` or `[` appear outside of quoted strings\n855\t/// in a line. This is used to distinguish between flow style mappings/sequences\n856\t/// and quoted strings that happen to contain these characters.\n857\t///\n858\t/// # Arguments\n859\t///\n860\t/// * `line` - The line to check\n861\t///\n862\t/// # Returns\n863\t///\n864\t/// `true` if unquoted `{` or `[` are found, `false` otherwise\n865\t///\n866\t/// # Examples\n867\t///\n868\t/// ```\n869\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::check_for_unquoted_brackets;\n870\t///\n871\t/// // Flow style mapping - has unquoted braces\n872\t/// assert!(check_for_unquoted_brackets(\"{key: value}\"));\n873\t///\n874\t/// // Flow style sequence - has unquoted brackets\n875\t/// assert!(check_for_unquoted_brackets(\"[item1, item2]\"));\n876\t///\n877\t/// // Quoted string with brackets - no unquoted brackets\n878\t/// assert!(!check_for_unquoted_brackets(\"key: \\\"value [with] brackets\\\"\"));\n879\t///\n880\t/// // Mixed - quoted brackets are OK, unquoted are not\n881\t/// assert!(check_for_unquoted_brackets(\"key: [value] \\\"more\\\"\"));\n882\t/// ```\n883\tfn check_for_unquoted_brackets(line: &str) -> bool {\n884\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n885\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n886\t    let mut escaped = false;\n887\t\n888\t    for ch in line.chars() {\n889\t        if escaped {\n890\t            escaped = false;\n891\t            continue;\n892\t        }\n893\t\n894\t        match ch {\n895\t            '\\\\' => {\n896\t                escaped = true;\n897\t            }\n898\t            '\\'' if !in_double_quote => {\n899\t                in_single_quote = !in_single_quote;\n900\t            }\n901\t            '\"' if !in_single_quote => {\n902\t                in_double_quote = !in_double_quote;\n903\t            }\n904\t            '[' if !in_single_quote && !in_double_quote => {\n905\t                return true; // Found unquoted opening bracket\n906\t            }\n907\t            '{' if !in_single_quote && !in_double_quote => {\n908\t                return true; // Found unquoted opening brace\n909\t            }\n910\t            _ => {}\n911\t        }\n912\t    }\n913\t\n914\t    false\n915\t}\n916\t\n917\t/// Find the first unquoted colon in a line\n918\t///\n919\t/// This helper function finds the first colon that is outside of quoted strings\n920\t/// and square brackets (for IPv6 addresses). This is used to locate the mapping\n921\t/// key separator in YAML lines.\n922\t///\n923\t/// # Arguments\n924\t///\n925\t/// * `line` - The line to search\n926\t///\n927\t/// # Returns\n928\t///\n929\t/// `Some(usize)` with the position of the first unquoted colon, or `None` if not found\n930\t///\n931\t/// # Examples\n932\t///\n933\t/// ```\n934\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::find_unquoted_colon;\n935\t///\n936\t/// // Simple key-value\n937\t/// assert_eq!(find_unquoted_colon(\"key: value\"), Some(3));\n938\t///\n939\t/// // Colon in quoted value\n940\t/// assert_eq!(find_unquoted_colon(\"time: \\\"12:30:00\\\"\"), Some(4));\n941\t///\n942\t/// // Quoted key with colon\n943\t/// assert_eq!(find_unquoted_colon(\"'key:with:colons': value\"), Some(17));\n944\t///\n945\t/// // IPv6 URL - colons inside brackets are skipped\n946\t/// assert_eq!(find_unquoted_colon(\"url: http://[2001:db8::1]:8080\"), Some(3));\n947\t///\n948\t/// // No colon\n949\t/// assert_eq!(find_unquoted_colon(\"no colon here\"), None);\n950\t/// ```\n951\tfn find_unquoted_colon(line: &str) -> Option<usize> {\n952\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n953\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n954\t    let mut in_brackets = 0; // Track nested brackets for IPv6 addresses\n955\t    let mut escaped = false;\n956\t\n957\t    for (pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n958\t        if escaped {\n959\t            escaped = false;\n960\t            continue;\n961\t        }\n962\t\n963\t        match ch {\n964\t            '\\\\' => {\n965\t                escaped = true;\n966\t            }\n967\t            '\\'' if !in_double_quote => {\n968\t                in_single_quote = !in_single_quote;\n969\t            }\n970\t            '\"' if !in_single_quote => {\n971\t                in_double_quote = !in_double_quote;\n972\t            }\n973\t            '[' if !in_single_quote && !in_double_quote => {\n974\t                in_brackets += 1;\n975\t            }\n976\t            ']' if !in_single_quote && !in_double_quote => {\n977\t                if in_brackets > 0 {\n978\t                    in_brackets -= 1;\n979\t                }\n980\t            }\n981\t            ':' if !in_single_quote && !in_double_quote && in_brackets == 0 => {\n982\t                return Some(pos); // Found unquoted colon outside of brackets\n983\t            }\n984\t            _ => {}\n985\t        }\n986\t    }\n987\t\n988\t    None\n989\t}\n990\t\n991\t/// Strip inline comment from a YAML line\n992\t///\n993\t/// This function removes inline comments from a YAML line while preserving:\n994\t/// - Hash characters (#) in URLs (http://example.com#anchor)\n995\t/// - Hash characters in quoted strings\n996\t/// - Hash characters in values\n997\t///\n998\t/// The function intelligently handles quoted strings and only removes comments\n999\t/// that are outside of quotes AND preceded by whitespace (per YAML spec).\n1000\t///\n1001\t/// # Arguments\n1002\t///\n1003\t/// * `line` - The line to process\n1004\t///\n1005\t/// # Returns\n1006\t///\n1007\t/// The line with inline comments removed, or the original line if no comments found\n1008\t///\n1009\t/// # Examples\n1010\t///\n1011\t/// ```\n1012\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::strip_inline_comment;\n1013\t///\n1014\t/// // Basic inline comment\n1015\t/// assert_eq!(strip_inline_comment(\"key: value # comment\"), \"key: value \");\n1016\t///\n1017\t/// // Comment without leading space\n1018\t/// assert_eq!(strip_inline_comment(\"key: value#comment\"), \"key: value\");\n1019\t///\n1020\t/// // Hash in URL should be preserved\n1021\t/// assert_eq!(strip_inline_comment(\"url: http://example.com#anchor\"), \"url: http://example.com#anchor\");\n1022\t///\n1023\t/// // Hash in quoted string should be preserved\n1024\t/// assert_eq!(strip_inline_comment(\"key: \\\"value with # hash\\\" # comment\"), \"key: \\\"value with # hash\\\" \");\n1025\t/// ```\n1026\tpub fn strip_inline_comment(line: &str) -> String {\n1027\t    let mut result = String::with_capacity(line.len());\n1028\t    let mut chars = line.chars().peekable();\n1029\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n1030\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n1031\t    let mut escaped = false;\n1032\t    let mut prev_char: Option<char> = None;\n1033\t\n1034\t    while let Some(ch) = chars.next() {\n1035\t        if escaped {\n1036\t            // After escape character, preserve everything\n1037\t            result.push(ch);\n1038\t            prev_char = Some(ch);\n1039\t            escaped = false;\n1040\t            continue;\n1041\t        }\n1042\t\n1043\t        match ch {\n1044\t            '\\\\' => {\n1045\t                result.push(ch);\n1046\t                prev_char = Some(ch);\n1047\t                escaped = true;\n1048\t            }\n1049\t            '\\'' if !in_double_quote => {\n1050\t                in_single_quote = !in_single_quote;\n1051\t                result.push(ch);\n1052\t                prev_char = Some(ch);\n1053\t            }\n1054\t            '\"' if !in_single_quote => {\n1055\t                in_double_quote = !in_double_quote;\n1056\t                result.push(ch);\n1057\t                prev_char = Some(ch);\n1058\t            }\n1059\t            '#' if !in_single_quote && !in_double_quote => {\n1060\t                // Check if this # is preceded by whitespace (YAML comment rule)\n1061\t                // A # only starts a comment if it's preceded by whitespace or at line start\n1062\t                let is_comment = prev_char.map_or(true, |c| c.is_whitespace());\n1063\t\n1064\t                if is_comment {\n1065\t                    // Found comment start - stop processing\n1066\t                    break;\n1067\t                } else {\n1068\t                    // # is part of the value (like in URL), preserve it\n1069\t                    result.push(ch);\n1070\t                    prev_char = Some(ch);\n1071\t                }\n1072\t            }\n1073\t            _ => {\n1074\t                result.push(ch);\n1075\t                prev_char = Some(ch);\n1076\t            }\n1077\t        }\n1078\t    }\n1079\t\n1080\t    result\n1081\t}\n1082\t\n1083\t/// Detect if a line is a YAML mapping key and extract key information\n1084\t///\n1085\t/// This function analyzes a YAML line to determine if it contains a mapping key,\n1086\t/// and extracts the key identifier and optional value. It handles various edge cases:\n1087\t///\n1088\t/// - Lines with colons in values (URLs, timestamps, etc.)\n1089\t/// - Comment lines with colons (excluded from detection)\n1090\t/// - Nested mappings (proper indentation)\n1091\t/// - Parent keys (keys without values on the same line)\n1092\t/// - Flow style mappings (excluded)\n1093\t/// - Special YAML constructs (anchors, aliases, tags - excluded)\n1094\t///\n1095\t/// # Arguments\n1096\t///\n1097\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n1098\t/// * `parent_indent` - The indentation level of the parent context (for nested mappings)\n1099\t///\n1100\t/// # Returns\n1101\t///\n1102\t/// - `Some(MappingKeyInfo)` if the line is a valid mapping key\n1103\t/// - `None` if the line is not a mapping key\n1104\t///\n1105\t/// # Examples\n1106\t///\n1107\t/// ```\n1108\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::detect_mapping_key;\n1109\t///\n1110\t/// // Simple key-value pair\n1111\t/// let info = detect_mapping_key(\"name: John\", 0);\n1112\t/// assert!(info.is_some());\n1113\t/// assert_eq!(info.unwrap().key, \"name\");\n1114\t///\n1115\t/// // Comment line with colon - should not be detected as key\n1116\t/// let info = detect_mapping_key(\"# This: is a comment\", 0);\n1117\t/// assert!(info.is_none());\n1118\t///\n1119\t/// // Key with colon in value (URL)\n1120\t/// let info = detect_mapping_key(\"url: http://example.com\", 0);\n1121\t/// assert!(info.is_some());\n1122\t/// assert_eq!(info.unwrap().key, \"url\");\n1123\t///\n1124\t/// // Parent key (no value on same line)\n1125\t/// let info = detect_mapping_key(\"nested:\", 0);\n1126\t/// assert!(info.is_some());\n1127\t/// assert_eq!(info.unwrap().key, \"nested\");\n1128\t/// assert!(info.unwrap().is_parent_key);\n1129\t/// ```\n1130\tpub fn detect_mapping_key(line: &str, parent_indent: usize) -> Option<MappingKeyInfo> {\n1131\t    // Check if this is a full-line comment before processing\n1132\t    if is_comment_line(line) {\n1133\t        return None;\n1134\t    }\n1135\t\n1136\t    // Strip inline comments before processing\n1137\t    let line_without_comments = strip_inline_comment(line);\n1138\t    let trimmed = line_without_comments.trim();\n1139\t\n1140\t    // Skip empty lines\n1141\t    if trimmed.is_empty() {\n1142\t        return None;\n1143\t    }\n1144\t\n1145\t    // Skip document markers\n1146\t    if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n1147\t        return None;\n1148\t    }\n1149\t\n1150\t    // Skip YAML directives\n1151\t    if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n1152\t        return None;\n1153\t    }\n1154\t\n1155\t    // Skip tags (start with !)\n1156\t    if trimmed.starts_with('!') {\n1157\t        return None;\n1158\t    }\n1159\t\n1160\t    // Skip anchors (start with &)\n1161\t    if trimmed.starts_with('&') {\n1162\t        return None;\n1163\t    }\n1164\t\n1165\t    // Skip aliases (start with *)\n1166\t    if trimmed.starts_with('*') {\n1167\t        return None;\n1168\t    }\n1169\t\n1170\t    // Handle sequence items - they are not mapping keys\n1171\t    if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") || trimmed == \"-\" {\n1172\t        // Sequence items should not be detected as mapping keys\n1173\t        return None;\n1174\t    }\n1175\t\n1176\t    let line_to_process = trimmed;\n1177\t\n1178\t    // Skip explicit key indicators (start with ?)\n1179\t    if line_to_process.starts_with('?') {\n1180\t        return None;\n1181\t    }\n1182\t\n1183\t    // Skip block scalar indicators\n1184\t    if trimmed.starts_with('|') || trimmed.starts_with('>') {\n1185\t        return None;\n1186\t    }\n1187\t\n1188\t    // Find the first unquoted colon in the line\n1189\t    // This skips colons inside quotes and brackets (for IPv6 addresses)\n1190\t    let colon_pos = find_unquoted_colon(line_to_process)?;\n1191\t\n1192\t    // Now check for flow style mappings/sequences more carefully\n1193\t    // We need to allow URLs with IPv6 addresses like \"key: http://[2001:db8::1]:8080\"\n1194\t    // But reject flow style like \"key: {value}\" or \"key: [items]\"\n1195\t\n1196\t    // Get the key and value parts\n1197\t    let key_part = &line_to_process[..colon_pos];\n1198\t    let value_part = if colon_pos + 1 < line_to_process.len() {\n1199\t        Some(line_to_process[colon_pos + 1..].trim())\n1200\t    } else {\n1201\t        None\n1202\t    };\n1203\t\n1204\t    // Check if key part contains { or [ (invalid for keys)\n1205\t    if check_for_unquoted_brackets(key_part) {\n1206\t        return None;\n1207\t    }\n1208\t\n1209\t    // Check if value is flow style (starts with { or [)\n1210\t    // But allow it if it looks like a URL (contains ://)\n1211\t    if let Some(value) = value_part {\n1212\t        let trimmed_value = value.trim();\n1213\t        if (trimmed_value.starts_with('{') || trimmed_value.starts_with('[')) && !trimmed_value.contains(\"://\") {\n1214\t            return None;\n1215\t        }\n1216\t    }\n1217\t\n1218\t    // Convert value_part to String for the final result\n1219\t    let value_part_string = value_part.map(|v| v.to_string());\n1220\t\n1221\t    // Trim the key part\n1222\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n1223\t\n1224\t    // Key must not be empty\n1225\t    if key.is_empty() {\n1226\t        return None;\n1227\t    }\n1228\t\n1229\t    // Check if this is a quoted key (single or double quotes)\n1230\t    let is_quoted_key = (key.starts_with('\\'') && key.ends_with('\\'') && key.len() > 1) ||\n1231\t                        (key.starts_with('\"') && key.ends_with('\"') && key.len() > 1);\n1232\t\n1233\t    // For non-quoted keys, validate that they only contain valid characters\n1234\t    // YAML keys can contain: alphanumeric, and most special characters except\n1235\t    // those with special meaning in YAML (colon, braces, brackets, commas, etc.)\n1236\t    if !is_quoted_key {\n1237\t        for ch in key.chars() {\n1238\t            // Allow alphanumeric and common safe characters\n1239\t            if ch.is_alphanumeric() || ch == '_' || ch == '.' || ch == '-' || ch == '!' {\n1240\t                continue;\n1241\t            }\n1242\t            // Reject characters with special YAML meaning\n1243\t            if ch == ':' || ch == '{' || ch == '}' || ch == '[' || ch == ']' || ch == ',' {\n1244\t                return None; // Invalid key character for unquoted key\n1245\t            }\n1246\t            // Reject YAML special markers\n1247\t            if ch == '&' || ch == '*' || ch == '?' || ch == '|' || ch == '>' || ch == '#' || ch == '%' || ch == '@' || ch == '`' {\n1248\t                return None; // These have special meaning in YAML\n1249\t            }\n1250\t            // Reject whitespace and control characters\n1251\t            if ch.is_whitespace() || ch.is_control() {\n1252\t                return None; // Invalid key character\n1253\t            }\n1254\t        }\n1255\t    }\n1256\t    // Quoted keys can contain any characters, so we skip validation for them\n1257\t\n1258\t    // Check for proper indentation relative to parent\n1259\t    let current_indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n1260\t\n1261\t    // Indentation validation rules:\n1262\t    // - current_indent < parent_indent: Invalid (exiting parent's context - not a child)\n1263\t    // - current_indent == parent_indent: Valid (sibling key at same level)\n1264\t    // - current_indent > parent_indent: Valid nested key IF:\n1265\t    //   - parent_indent > 0 AND indent_increase >= 2 (proper nesting)\n1266\t    //   - OR parent_indent == 0 (root level, any indentation is valid)\n1267\t\n1268\t    if current_indent < parent_indent {\n1269\t        // Decreasing indentation means we've exited the parent's context\n1270\t        // This line is not a child of the current parent\n1271\t        return None;\n1272\t    } else if current_indent > parent_indent {\n1273\t        let indent_increase = current_indent - parent_indent;\n1274\t\n1275\t        // When parent is at root level (0), be lenient - allow any positive indent\n1276\t        // When nested, require at least 2 spaces for proper nesting\n1277\t        if parent_indent > 0 && indent_increase < 2 {\n1278\t            return None; // Insufficient indentation for nested key\n1279\t        }\n1280\t    }\n1281\t    // current_indent == parent_indent is valid - sibling key at same level\n1282\t\n1283\t    // Check if this is a parent key (no value or just whitespace after colon)\n1284\t    let is_parent = value_part_string.as_ref().map_or(true, |v| v.is_empty() || v.starts_with('#'));\n1285\t    let value = if is_parent {\n1286\t        None\n1287\t    } else {\n1288\t        value_part_string\n1289\t    };\n1290\t\n1291\t    Some(MappingKeyInfo::new(key.to_string(), value))\n1292\t}\n1293\t\n1294\t/// Combined function to detect mapping key and return key with child indentation\n1295\t///\n1296\t/// This is a convenience wrapper that integrates all detection logic and returns\n1297\t/// a simple `(String, usize)` tuple containing the key name and the child's indentation level.\n1298\t///\n1299\t/// This function handles all edge cases including:\n1300\t/// - Quoted values with colons: `message: \"Hello: World\"`\n1301\t/// - URLs with colons: `url: http://example.com`\n1302\t/// - Time values: `time: 10:30:00`\n1303\t/// - Nested mappings: `parent:\\n  child: value`\n1304\t/// - Comment filtering: `# comment: text` is rejected\n1305\t/// - Special constructs: sequences, anchors, aliases are rejected\n1306\t///\n1307\t/// # Arguments\n1308\t///\n1309\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n1310\t/// * `parent_indent` - The indentation level of the parent context\n1311\t///\n1312\t/// # Returns\n1313\t///\n1314\t/// - `Some((String, usize))` where the String is the key name and usize is the child's indentation level\n1315\t/// - `None` if the line is not a valid mapping key\n1316\t///\n1317\t/// # Examples\n1318\t///\n1319\t/// ```\n1320\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::detect_mapping_key_simple;\n1321\t///\n1322\t/// // Simple key-value pair\n1323\t/// let result = detect_mapping_key_simple(\"name: John\", 0);\n1324\t/// assert_eq!(result, Some((\"name\".to_string(), 0)));\n1325\t///\n1326\t/// // Nested key\n1327\t/// let result = detect_mapping_key_simple(\"  child: value\", 0);\n1328\t/// assert_eq!(result, Some((\"child\".to_string(), 2)));\n1329\t///\n1330\t/// // Comment line - rejected\n1331\t/// let result = detect_mapping_key_simple(\"# comment: text\", 0);\n1332\t/// assert_eq!(result, None);\n1333\t///\n1334\t/// // URL with colon\n1335\t/// let result = detect_mapping_key_simple(\"url: http://example.com\", 0);\n1336\t/// assert_eq!(result, Some((\"url\".to_string(), 0)));\n1337\t/// ```\n1338\tpub fn detect_mapping_key_simple(line: &str, parent_indent: usize) -> Option<(String, usize)> {\n1339\t    detect_mapping_key(line, parent_indent).map(|info| {\n1340\t        let child_indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n1341\t        (info.key, child_indent)\n1342\t    })\n1343\t}\n1344\t\n1345\t/// Result of parsing a YAML file into lines\n1346\t///\n1347\t/// This type contains the complete results of a line-by-line YAML parsing operation.\n1348\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n1349\tpub struct LineParseResult {\n1350\t    /// All parsed lines in order\n1351\t    pub lines: Vec<YamlLine>,\n1352\t\n1353\t    /// Total number of lines processed\n1354\t    pub total_lines: usize,\n1355\t\n1356\t    /// Number of blank lines found\n1357\t    pub blank_lines: usize,\n1358\t\n1359\t    /// Number of comment lines found\n1360\t    pub comment_lines: usize,\n1361\t\n1362\t    /// Number of content lines found\n1363\t    pub content_lines: usize,\n1364\t\n1365\t    /// Maximum indentation level found\n1366\t    pub max_indentation: usize,\n1367\t}\n1368\t\n1369\timpl LineParseResult {\n1370\t    /// Create a new line parse result from a vector of lines\n1371\t    ///\n1372\t    /// # Arguments\n1373\t    ///\n1374\t    /// * `lines` - The parsed YAML lines\n1375\t    ///\n1376\t    /// # Returns\n1377\t    /// A new `LineParseResult` with computed statistics\n1378\t    pub fn new(lines: Vec<YamlLine>) -> Self {\n1379\t        let total_lines = lines.len();\n1380\t        let blank_lines = lines.iter().filter(|l| l.is_blank()).count();\n1381\t        let comment_lines = lines.iter().filter(|l| l.is_comment()).count();\n1382\t        let content_lines = lines.iter().filter(|l| l.is_content()).count();\n1383\t        let max_indentation = lines.iter().map(|l| l.indentation_level()).max().unwrap_or(0);\n1384\t\n1385\t        Self {\n1386\t            lines,\n1387\t            total_lines,\n1388\t            blank_lines,\n1389\t            comment_lines,\n1390\t            content_lines,\n1391\t            max_indentation,\n1392\t        }\n1393\t    }\n1394\t\n1395\t    /// Check if the parse result is empty\n1396\t    ///\n1397\t    /// # Returns\n1398\t    /// `true` if no lines were parsed\n1399\t    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n1400\t        self.lines.is_empty()\n1401\t    }\n1402\t\n1403\t    /// Get lines by type\n1404\t    ///\n1405\t    /// # Arguments\n1406\t    ///\n1407\t    /// * `line_type` - The line type to filter by\n1408\t    ///\n1409\t    /// # Returns\n1410\t    /// An iterator over lines matching the specified type\n1411\t    pub fn lines_by_type(&self, line_type: LineType) -> impl Iterator<Item = &YamlLine> {\n1412\t        self.lines.iter().filter(move |line| line.line_type() == line_type)\n1413\t    }\n1414\t\n1415\t    /// Get content lines (non-structural)\n1416\t    ///\n1417\t    /// # Returns\n1418\t    /// An iterator over content lines\n1419\t    pub fn content_lines(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &YamlLine> {\n1420\t        self.lines.iter().filter(|line| line.is_content())\n1421\t    }\n1422\t\n1423\t    /// Get structural lines (blank, comments, document markers)\n1424\t    ///\n1425\t    /// # Returns\n1426\t    /// An iterator over structural lines\n1427\t    pub fn structural_lines(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &YamlLine> {\n1428\t        self.lines.iter().filter(|line| line.is_structural())\n1429\t    }\n1430\t}\n1431\t\n1432\t// Include the comprehensive exclamation mark tests\n1433\t#[cfg(test)]\n1434\t#[path = \"exclamation_mark_tests.rs\"]\n1435\tmod exclamation_mark_tests;\n1436\t\n1437\t#[cfg(test)]\n1438\tmod tests {\n1439\t    use super::*;\n1440\t\n1441\t    #[test]\n1442\t    fn test_line_type_descriptions() {\n1443\t        assert_eq!(LineType::Blank.description(), \"blank line\");\n1444\t        assert_eq!(LineType::Comment.description(), \"comment line\");\n1445\t        assert_eq!(LineType::MappingKey.description(), \"mapping key\");\n1446\t    }\n1447\t\n1448\t    #[test]\n1449\t    fn test_line_type_classification() {\n1450\t      let line = YamlLine::new(1, \"key: value\", 0, LineType::MappingKey);\n1460\t        assert_eq!(line.line_number(), 1);\n1461\t        assert_eq!(line.raw_content(), \"key: value\");\n1462\t        assert_eq!(line.indentation_level(), 0);\n1463\t        assert_eq!(line.line_type(), LineType::MappingKey);\n1464\t    }\n1465\t\n1466\t    #[test]\n1467\t    fn test_yaml_line_with_content() assert_eq!(result.max_indentation, 2);\n1518\t    }\n1519\t\n1520\t    #[test]\n1521\t    fn test_line_parse_result_filtering() {\n1522\t        let lines = vec![\n1523\t            YamlLine::new(1, ];\n1527\t\n1528\t        let result = LineParseResult::new(lines);\n1529\t\n1530\t        let content_lines: Vec<_> = result.content_lines().collect();\n1531\t        assert_eq!(content_lines.len(), tests\n1538\t    #[test]\n1539\t    fn test_indentation_info_no_indentation() {\n1540\t        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"key: value\");\n1541\t        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces,       assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n1768\t    }\n1769\t\n1770\t    // Integration tests combining classification and indentation\n1771\t    #[test]\n1772\t    fn test_classification_and_indentation_integration() {\n1773\t        // Test various YAML patterns together\n1774\t        let lines = vec![\n1775\t            (\"\", LineType::Blank, 0),\n1776\t            (\"   \", LineType::Blank, 3),\n1777\t            (\"# comment\", LineType::Comment, 0),\n1778\t            (\"  # comment\", LineType::Comment, 2),\n1779\t            (\"---\", LineType::DocumentStart, 0),\n1780\t            (\"  ---\", LineType::DocumentStart, 2),\n1781\t            (\"...\", LineType::DocumentEnd, 0),\n1782\t            (\"key: value\", LineType::MappingKey, 0),\n1783\t            (\"  key: value\", LineType::MappingKey, 2),\n1784\t            (\"    key: value\", LineType::MappingKey, 4),\n1785\t            (\"- item\", LineType::SequenceItem, 0),\n1786\t            (\"  - item\", LineType::SequenceItem, 2),\n1787\t            (\"|\", LineType::LiteralBlockScalar, 0),\n1788\t            (\"  >\", LineType::FoldedBlockScalar, 2),\n1789\t        ];\n1790\t\n1791\t        for (line, expected_type, expected_indent) in lines {\n1792\t            assert_eq!(\n1793\t                classify_line_type(line),\n1794\t                expected_type,\n1795\t                \"Failed to classify line: '{}'\",\n1796\t                line\n1797\t            );\n1798\t            assert_eq!(\n1799\t                calculate_indentation(line),\n1800\t                expected_indent,\n1801\t                \"Failed to calculate indentation for line: '{}'\",\n1802\t                line\n1803\t            );\n1804\t        }\n1805\t    }\n1806\t\n1807\t    #[test]\n1808\t    fn test_indentation_info_with_various_yaml_patterns() {\n1809\t        // Test indentation info with realistic YAML patterns\n1810\t        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"  - item\");\n1811\t        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 2);\n1812\t        assert_eq!(info.leading_tabs, 0);\n1813\t        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 2);\n1814\t\n1815\t        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"\\t- item\");\n1816\t        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 0);\n1817\t        assert_eq!(info.leading_tabs, 1);\n1818\t        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 1);\n1819\t\n1820\t        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"    key: value\");\n1821\t        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 4);\n1822\t        assert_eq!(info.leading_tabs, 0);\n1823\t        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 4);\n1824\t    }\n1825\t\n1826\t    // Mapping key detection test_detect_mapping_key_complex_nested_structure() {\n2174\t        // Test complex nested structure\n2175\t        let lines = vec![\n2176\t            (\"root:\", 0, true, false),    // parent key\n2177\t            (\"  child1: value1\", 0, true, true),\n2178\t            (\"  child2:\", 0, true, false), // parent key\n2179\t            (\"    grandchild: value2\", 2, true, true),\n2180\t            (\"  child3: value3\", 0, true, true),\n2181\t        ];\n2182\t\n2183\t        for (line, parent_indent, expected_some, expected_has_value) in lines {\n2184\t            let info = detect_mapping_key(line, parent_indent);\n2185\t            assert_eq!(info.is_some(), expected_some, \"Failed for line: {}\", line);\n2186\t            if expected_some {\n2187\t                let info = info.unwrap();\n2188\t                assert_eq!(info.has_inline_value, expected_has_value, \"Failed for line: {}\", line);\n2189\t            }\n2190\t        }\n2191\t    }\n2192\t\n2193\t
