assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(&very_long_indent), 200);\n\n        let extremely_long_indent = \" \".repeat(500) + \"key: value\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(&extremely_long_indent), 500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_hash_only() {\n        // Lines with only \"#\" symbol (no text after)\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"#\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t#\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"    #\"), LineType::Comment);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_whitespace_and_hash() {\n        // Lines with only whitespace and \"#\"\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"   #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t#\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  \\t #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t  #\"), LineType::Comment);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_unicode_content() {\n        // Unicode content with indentation\n        let unicode_line = \"  clé: valeur\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line), 2);\n\n        let unicode_line2 = \"    名前: 値\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line2), 4);\n\n        let unicode_line3 = \"\\t用户名: 密码\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line3), 1);\n\n        let unicode_line4 = \"      ελληνικά: Greek\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line4), 6);\n\n        let unicode_line5 = \"    🎨: emoji\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line5), 4);\n\n        let unicode_line6 = \"  ñoño: value\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line6), 2);\n\n        let unicode_line7 = \"    -key-with-accents: value\";\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(unicode_line7), 4);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_mixed_whitespace() {\n        // Mixed whitespace in comment lines\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t# comment\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  \\t# comment\"), 3);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t # comment\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t # comment\"), 3);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  \\t  # comment\"), 5);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t \\t# comment\"), 3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indentation_info_very_long_indentation() {\n        // Very long indentation (100+ spaces)\n        let long_indent = \" \".repeat(100) + \"key: value\";\n        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(&long_indent);\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 100);\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_tabs, 0);\n        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 100);\n        assert!(info.is_spaces_only());\n        assert!(!info.is_mixed());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indentation_info_mixed_whitespace_variations() {\n        // Various mixed whitespace patterns\n        let info1 = IndentationInfo::from_line(\" \\t#\");\n        assert_eq!(info1.leading_spaces, 1);\n        assert_eq!(info1.leading_tabs, 1);\n        assert!(info1.is_mixed());\n\n        let info2 = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"  \\t#\");\n        assert_eq!(info2.leading_spaces, 2);\n        assert_eq!(info2.leading_tabs, 1);\n        assert!(info2.is_mixed());\n\n        let info3 = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"\\t #\");\n        assert_eq!(info3.leading_spaces, 1);\n        assert_eq!(info3.leading_tabs, 1);\n        assert!(info3.is_mixed());\n\n        let info4 = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"\\t\\t  #\");\n        assert_eq!(info4.leading_spaces, 2);\n        assert_eq!(info4.leading_tabs, 2);\n        assert!(info4.is_mixed());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_unicode_with_indentation() {\n        // Unicode content classification with various indentation levels\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  clé: valeur\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"    名前: 値\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t用户名: 密码\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  # комментарий\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"    # תגובה\"), LineType::Comment);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_mixed_whitespace_with_content() {\n        // Mixed whitespace with different content types\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t key: value\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t # comment\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  \\t - item\"), LineType::SequenceItem);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t #\"), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t  \"), LineType::Blank);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_empty_lines_edge_cases() {\n        // Empty and whitespace-only edge cases\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\"), 0);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \"), 1);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  \"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t\"), 1);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t\\t\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t \"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t \"), 3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_calculate_indentation_only_comments_edge_cases() {\n        // Comments with various indentation patterns\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"#\"), 0);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" #\"), 1);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  #\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t#\"), 1);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\" \\t#\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"\\t #\"), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"    # comment\"), 4);\n        assert_eq!(calculate_indentation(\"  \\t # deep comment\"), 4);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indentation_info_unicode_edge_cases() {\n        // Unicode with various edge cases\n        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"  👍: thumbs up\");\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 2);\n        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 2);\n\n        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"    🔥: fire\");\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_spaces, 4);\n        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 4);\n\n        let info = IndentationInfo::from_line(\"\\t🌍: world\");\n        assert_eq!(info.leading_tabs, 1);\n        assert_eq!(info.total_level, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_classify_line_type_combined_edge_cases() {\n        // Combined edge case testing\n        // Very long indentation with various content types\n        let very_long_indent = \" \".repeat(100);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}key: value\", very_long_indent)), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}# comment\", very_long_indent)), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}- item\", very_long_indent)), LineType::SequenceItem);\n\n        // Hash only with various indentation\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}#\", \" \".repeat(50))), LineType::Comment);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(&format!(\"{}#\", \"\\t\".repeat(10))), LineType::Comment);\n\n        // Mixed whitespace with unicode\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\" \\t clé: valeur\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"  \\t 用户名: 密码\"), LineType::MappingKey);\n        assert_eq!(classify_line_type(\"\\t \\t # 注释\"), LineType::Comment);\n    }\n\n    // Integration tests for comment filtering in key detection\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_skips_full_line_comments() {\n        // Full-line comments should be ignored\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"# This is a comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Full-line comment should not be detected as key\");\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"  # Indented comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Indented full-line comment should not be detected as key\");\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"\\t# Tab-indented comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Tab-indented comment should not be detected as key\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_strips_inline_comments() {\n        // Inline comments should be stripped before processing\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"key: value # inline comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with inline comment should still be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"key\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"value\".to_string()));\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"  nested: value # comment here\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Nested key with inline comment should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"nested\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"value\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_preserves_hashes_in_values() {\n        // Hashes in URLs and values should be preserved\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"url: http://example.com#anchor\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with URL hash should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"url\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"http://example.com#anchor\".to_string()));\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"link: https://api.example.com/v1#section # comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with URL hash and inline comment should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"link\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"https://api.example.com/v1#section\".to_string()));\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"text: value # hash # in # value\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with hash characters in value should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        // The first # preceded by whitespace starts the comment, so value should be \"value\"\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"value\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_preserves_quoted_hashes() {\n        // Hashes in quoted strings should be preserved\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"key: \\\"value with # hash\\\"\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with quoted hash should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"\\\"value with # hash\\\"\".to_string()));\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"key: 'value #2' # inline comment\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Key with quoted hash and inline comment should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"'value #2'\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_comment_lines_with_colons_ignored() {\n        // Comment lines containing colons should be ignored\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"# TODO: fix this bug\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Comment line with colon should not be detected as key\");\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"  # Note: this is important\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Indented comment line with colon should not be detected as key\");\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"# http://example.com#anchor\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_none(), \"Comment line with URL should not be detected as key\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_key_detection_non_comment_lines_unchanged() {\n        // Regular non-comment lines should work as before\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"key: value\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Regular key-value pair should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(info.key, \"key\");\n        assert_eq!(info.value, Some(\"value\".to_string()));\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"  nested: value\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Nested key should be detected\");\n\n        let info = detect_mapping_key(\"parent:\", 0);\n        assert!(info.is_some(), \"Parent key should be detected\");\n        let info = info.unwrap();\n        assert!(info.is_parent_key);\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_8ea5288575ca433ca9a969b8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope representation data structures\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!\n51\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n52\t//!\n53\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n54\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n55\t//!\n56\t//! // Add keys to current scope\n57\t//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n58\t//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n59\t//!\n60\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n61\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n62\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n63\t//! ```\n64\t\n65\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n66\tuse std::fmt;\n67\t\n68\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n69\tuse log::debug as log_debug;\n70\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n71\tuse log::warn as log_warn;\n72\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n73\tuse log::trace as log_trace;\n74\t\n75\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n76\t///\n77\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n78\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n79\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n80\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n81\tpub struct Scope {\n82\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n83\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n84\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n85\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n86\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n87\t    pub start_line: usize,\n88\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n89\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n90\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n91\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n92\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n93\t    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n94\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n95\t    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\timpl Scope {\n99\t    /// Create a new scope\n100\t    ///\n101\t    /// # Arguments\n102\t    ///\n103\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n104\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n105\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n106\t    ///\n107\t    /// # Examples\n108\t    ///\n109\t    /// ```\n110\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n111\t    ///\n112\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n113\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n114\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n115\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n116\t    /// ```\n117\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n118\t        Self {\n119\t            indent_level,\n120\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n121\t            start_line,\n122\t            parent_key,\n123\t            is_flow_style: false,\n124\t            in_sequence_context: false,\n125\t            sequence_item_id: None,\n126\t        }\n127\t    }\n128\t\n129\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n130\t    ///\n131\t    /// # Arguments\n132\t    ///\n133\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n134\t    ///\n135\t    /// # Returns\n136\t    ///\n137\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n138\t    ///\n139\t    /// # Examples\n140\t    ///\n141\t    /// ```\n142\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n143\t    ///\n144\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n145\t    ///\n146\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n147\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n148\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n149\t    /// ```\n150\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n151\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n155\t    ///\n156\t    /// # Arguments\n157\t    ///\n158\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\n159\t    ///\n160\t    /// # Returns\n161\t    ///\n162\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n163\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n164\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n168\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n169\t        self.keys.len()\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n173\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n174\t        self.keys.clear();\n175\t    }\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n179\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n180\t        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n181\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n182\t            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n183\t        }\n184\t        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n185\t    }\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n189\t///\n190\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n191\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n192\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n193\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n194\tpub struct ScopeStack {\n195\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n196\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n197\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n198\t    base_indent: usize,\n199\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n200\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\timpl ScopeStack {\n204\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n205\t    ///\n206\t    /// # Arguments\n207\t    ///\n208\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n209\t    ///\n210\t    /// # Examples\n211\t    ///\n212\t    /// ```\n213\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n216\t    /// ```\n217\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n218\t        Self {\n219\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n220\t            base_indent,\n221\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n222\t        }\n223\t    }\n224\t\n225\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n226\t    ///\n227\t    /// # Panics\n228\t    ///\n229\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n230\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n231\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n235\t    ///\n236\t    /// # Panics\n237\t    ///\n238\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n239\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n240\t        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n241\t    }\n242\t\n243\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n244\t    ///\n245\t    /// # Arguments\n246\t    ///\n247\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n248\t    ///\n249\t    /// # Returns\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n252\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n253\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n254\t    }\n255\t\n256\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n257\t    ///\n258\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n259\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n260\t    ///\n261\t    /// # Arguments\n262\t    ///\n263\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n264\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n265\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n266\t    ///\n267\t    /// # Examples\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// ```\n270\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n271\t    ///\n272\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n273\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n274\t    /// ```\n275\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n276\t        // Debug logging for scope entry\n277\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n278\t        {\n279\t            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n280\t            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n281\t            log_debug!(\n282\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n283\t                scope_type,\n284\t                line,\n285\t                indent_level,\n286\t                parent_info,\n287\t                self.depth(),\n288\t                self.get_scope_path()\n289\t            );\n290\t        }\n291\t\n292\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n293\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n294\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n295\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n296\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n297\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n298\t\n299\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n300\t            let before_depth = self.depth();\n301\t            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n302\t\n303\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n304\t            {\n305\t                let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n306\t                    .filter(|s| s.indent_level > indent_level)\n307\t                    .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n308\t                    .collect();\n309\t                if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n310\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removing {} scopes: {:?}\",\n311\t                             scopes_to_remove.len(),\n312\t                             scopes_to_remove);\n313\t                }\n314\t            }\n315\t\n316\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n317\t\n318\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n319\t            {\n320\t                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\n321\t                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\n322\t                if removed_count > 0 {\n323\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n324\t                             removed_count,\n325\t                             before_path,\n326\t                             self.get_scope_path());\n327\t                }\n328\t            }\n329\t\n330\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n331\t\n332\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n333\t            {\n334\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n335\t            }\n336\t        } else {\n337\t            // Create new scope\n338\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n339\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n340\t\n341\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n342\t            {\n343\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\", indent_level);\n344\t            }\n345\t        }\n346\t\n347\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n348\t        {\n349\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\n350\t        }\n351\t    }\n352\t\n353\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n354\t    ///\n355\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n356\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n357\t    ///\n358\t    /// # Arguments\n359\t    ///\n360\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n361\t    ///\n362\t    /// # Examples\n363\t    ///\n364\t    /// ```\n365\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n366\t    ///\n367\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n368\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n369\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n370\t    /// ```\n371\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n372\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n373\t        {\n374\t            log_debug!(\n375\t                \"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\",\n376\t                target_indent,\n377\t                self.depth(),\n378\t                self.current_indent(),\n379\t                self.get_scope_path()\n380\t            );\n381\t        }\n382\t\n383\t        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\n384\t        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\n385\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n386\t            {\n387\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\n388\t            }\n389\t            return;\n390\t        }\n391\t\n392\t        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\n393\t        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\n394\t            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n395\t            .count() == 0;\n396\t\n397\t        if would_be_empty {\n398\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n399\t            {\n400\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\");\n401\t            }\n402\t            // Keep at least the root scope\n403\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\n404\t            return;\n405\t        }\n406\t\n407\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n408\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n409\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n410\t\n411\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n412\t        {\n413\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\n414\t        }\n415\t\n416\t        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n417\t        // Check if there's a scope at target_indent + base_indent (for parent key scopes)\n418\t        let adjusted_target = target_indent + self.base_indent;\n419\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n420\t            // Check if there's a scope at the adjusted level (parent key scope)\n421\t            if self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == adjusted_target) {\n422\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n423\t                {\n424\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Found scope at adjusted indent={}, no fallback needed\", adjusted_target);\n425\t                }\n426\t                // Scope exists at adjusted level, no need for fallback\n427\t            } else {\n428\t                // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n429\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n430\t                {\n431\t                    log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at indent={} or adjusted_indent={}, creating fallback scope at target_indent\", target_indent, adjusted_target);\n432\t                }\n433\t                let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n434\t                self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n435\t            }\n436\t        }\n437\t\n438\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n439\t        {\n440\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\n441\t        }\n442\t    }\n443\t\n444\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n445\t    ///\n446\t    /// # Arguments\n447\t    ///\n448\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n449\t    ///\n450\t    /// # Returns\n451\t    ///\n452\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n453\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n454\t        self.scopes.last()\n455\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n456\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n457\t    }\n458\t\n459\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n460\t    ///\n461\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n462\t    ///\n463\t    /// # Arguments\n464\t    ///\n465\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n466\t    ///\n467\t    /// # Returns\n468\t    ///\n469\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n470\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n471\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n472\t    }\n473\t\n474\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n475\t    ///\n476\t    /// # Arguments\n477\t    ///\n478\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n479\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n480\t    ///\n481\t    /// # Returns\n482\t    ///\n483\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n484\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n485\t    ///\n486\t    /// # Examples\n487\t    ///\n488\t    /// ```\n489\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n490\t    ///\n491\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n492\t    ///\n493\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n494\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n495\t    ///\n496\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\n497\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n498\t    /// ```\n499\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n500\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n501\t\n502\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n503\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n504\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n505\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n506\t                key: key.to_string(),\n507\t                scope_path,\n508\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n509\t                duplicate_line: line,\n510\t            })\n511\t        } else {\n512\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n513\t            scope.add_key(key);\n514\t            Ok(())\n515\t        }\n516\t    }\n517\t\n518\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n519\t    ///\n520\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n521\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n522\t    ///\n523\t    /// # Returns\n524\t    ///\n525\t    /// A string representing the scope path\n526\t    ///\n527\t    /// # Examples\n528\t    ///\n529\t    /// ```\n530\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n531\t    ///\n532\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n533\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n534\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n535\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n536\t    /// ```\n537\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n538\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n539\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n540\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n541\t                path.push(key.clone());\n542\t            }\n543\t        }\n544\t        path.join(\".\")\n545\t    }\n546\t\n547\t    /// Get current indent level\n548\t    ///\n549\t    /// # Returns\n550\t    ///\n551\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n552\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n553\t        self.scopes.last()\n554\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n555\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n556\t    }\n557\t\n558\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n559\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n560\t        self.scopes.len()\n561\t    }\n562\t\n563\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n564\t    ///\n565\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n566\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n567\t        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n568\t    }\n569\t\n570\t    /// Get the base indentation size\n571\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n572\t        self.base_indent\n573\t    }\n574\t\n575\t    /// Set the base indentation size\n576\t    ///\n577\t    /// # Arguments\n578\t    ///\n579\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n580\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n581\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n582\t    }\n583\t\n584\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n585\t    ///\n586\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n587\t    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n588\t    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n589\t    ///\n590\t    /// # Arguments\n591\t    ///\n592\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n593\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n594\t    ///\n595\t    /// # Examples\n596\t    ///\n597\t    /// ```\n598\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n599\t    ///\n600\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n601\t    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n602\t    /// ```\n603\t    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n604\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n605\t        {\n606\t            log_debug!(\n607\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n608\t                line,\n609\t                indent_level,\n610\t                self.depth(),\n611\t                self.get_scope_path()\n612\t            );\n613\t        }\n614\t\n615\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level, but preserve parent mapping scopes\n616\t        // When entering a sequence scope, we need to keep the parent mapping scope intact\n617\t        // while clearing any nested scopes from previous content\n618\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n619\t        let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n620\t\n621\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n622\t        {\n623\t            let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n624\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level >= indent_level && s.parent_key.is_none())\n625\t                .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n626\t                .collect();\n627\t            if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n628\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removing {} non-parent scopes: {:?}\",\n629\t                         scopes_to_remove.len(),\n630\t                         scopes_to_remove);\n631\t            }\n632\t        }\n633\t\n634\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level || s.parent_key.is_some());\n635\t\n636\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n637\t        {\n638\t            let removed_count = before_depth - self.depth();\n639\t            if removed_count > 0 {\n640\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n641\t                         removed_count,\n642\t                         before_path,\n643\t                         self.get_scope_path());\n644\t            }\n645\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level, preserved parent mappings\", indent_level);\n646\t        }\n647\t\n648\t        // Check if there's already a scope at this level that's in a sequence context\n649\t        let needs_new_scope = self.scopes.last()\n650\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level != indent_level || !scope.in_sequence_context)\n651\t            .unwrap_or(true);\n652\t\n653\t        if needs_new_scope {\n654\t            // Create a new scope for this sequence item\n655\t            let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n656\t            new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n657\t            self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n658\t            new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n659\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n660\t\n661\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n662\t            {\n663\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n664\t            }\n665\t        } else {\n666\t            // Reset the existing scope for a new sequence item\n667\t            if let Some(scope) = self.scopes.last_mut() {\n668\t                scope.keys.clear();\n669\t                scope.start_line = line;\n670\t                self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n671\t                scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n672\t\n673\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n674\t                {\n675\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (reset), item_id={}, reset existing sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n676\t                }\n677\t            }\n678\t        }\n679\t\n680\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n681\t        {\n682\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\n683\t        }\n684\t    }\n685\t\n686\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n687\t    ///\n688\t    /// # Returns\n689\t    ///\n690\t    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n691\t    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n692\t        self.scopes.last()\n693\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n694\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n695\t    }\n696\t}\n697\t\n698\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n699\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n700\t        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n701\t               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n702\t    }\n703\t}\n704\t\n705\t/// classification\n765\t///\n766\t/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n767\t/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n768\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n769\tpub enum KeyContext {\n770\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n771\t    ///\n772\t    /// The key has a simple scalar value on items\n791\t    ParentSequence {\n792\t        /// The parent key name\n793\t        key: String,\n794\t    },\n795\t}\n796\t\n797\timpl KeyContext {\n798\t    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n799\t    ///\n800\t    /// # Returns\n801\t    ///\n802\t    /// The key name as a string slice\n803\t    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n804\t        match self {\n805\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n806\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n807\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n808\t        }\n809\t    }\n810\t\n811\t    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n812\t    ///\n813\t    /// # Returns\n814\t    ///\n815\t    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n816\t    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n817\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n818\t    }\n819\t\n820\t    /// Check if KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n840\t                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n841\t            }\n842\t        }\n843\t    }\n844\t}\n845\t\n846\t/// Extract key context from a line\n847\t///\n848\t/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n849\t/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n850\t///\n851\t/// # Arguments\n852\t///\n853\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n854\t///\n855\t/// # Returns\n856\t///\n857\t/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n858\t///\n859\t/// # Examples\n860\t///\n861\t/// ```\n862\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n863\t///\n864\t/// // Inline scalar\n865\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n866\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n867\t///\n868\t/// // Parent mapping\n869\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n870\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n871\t/// ```\n872\tpub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n873\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\n874\t\n875\t    // Find colon position\n876\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n877\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n878\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n879\t\n880\t    // Skip if key is empty\n881\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n882\t    if key.is_empty() {\n883\t        return None;\n884\t    }\n885\t\n886\t    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n887\t    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n888\t        return None;\n889\t    }\n890\t\n891\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n892\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n893\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n894\t    } else {\n895\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n896\t            key: key.to_string(),\n897\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n898\t        }\n899\t    };\n900\t\n901\t    Some(context)\n902\t}\n903\t\n904\t/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n905\t///\n906\t/// # Arguments\n907\t///\n908\t/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n909\t///\n910\t/// # Returns\n911\t///\n912\t/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n913\t///\n914\t/// # Examples\n915\t///\n916\t/// ```\n917\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n918\t///\n919\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n920\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n921\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n922\t/// ```\n923\tpub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n924\t    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n925\t}\n926\t\n927\t// Comprehensive tests are in the separate tests.rs file\n928\t#[cfg(test)]\n929\tmod tests;\n930\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"eb5a7fe0-4ca2-495e-b374-fef10f57d14f","uuid":"9c6bb7d4-217c-4868-8ac0-99c394070fd3","timestamp":"2026-07-13T19:39:36.886Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs","content":"//! Scope representation data structures\n//!\n//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n//!\n//! # Overview\n//!\n//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! services:\n//!   web:\n//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n//!     port: 8080\n//!   database:\n//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n//!     port: 5432\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The scope representation system tracks:\n//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n//!\n//! # Architecture\n//!\n//! ## Scope\n//!\n//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n//! - Line number where this scope started\n//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n//!\n//! ## ScopeStack\n//!\n//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n//!\n//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n//!\n//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n//!\n//! // Add keys to current scope\n//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n//!\n//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n//! ```\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::warn as log_warn;\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::trace as log_trace;\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n///\n/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    pub indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n            in_sequence_context: false,\n            sequence_item_id: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.keys.contains(key)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.keys.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n        self.keys.clear();\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n        }\n        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n///\n/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Create a new scope stack\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n            base_indent,\n            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n        // Debug logging for scope entry\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n            log_debug!(\n                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n                scope_type,\n                line,\n                indent_level,\n                parent_info,\n                self.depth(),\n                self.get_scope_path()\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n\n            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n            let before_depth = self.depth();\n            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n                    .filter(|s| s.indent_level > indent_level)\n                    .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n                    .collect();\n                if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removing {} scopes: {:?}\",\n                             scopes_to_remove.len(),\n                             scopes_to_remove);\n                }\n            }\n\n            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\n                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\n                if removed_count > 0 {\n                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n                             removed_count,\n                             before_path,\n                             self.get_scope_path());\n                }\n            }\n\n            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n            }\n        } else {\n            // Create new scope\n            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\", indent_level);\n            }\n        }\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n    /// ```\n    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\n                \"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\",\n                target_indent,\n                self.depth(),\n                self.current_indent(),\n                self.get_scope_path()\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\n        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\n            }\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\n        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\n            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n            .count() == 0;\n\n        if would_be_empty {\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\");\n            }\n            // Keep at least the root scope\n            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n        let before_depth = self.depth();\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\n        }\n\n        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n        // Check if there's a scope at target_indent + base_indent (for parent key scopes)\n        let adjusted_target = target_indent + self.base_indent;\n        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n            // Check if there's a scope at the adjusted level (parent key scope)\n            if self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == adjusted_target) {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Found scope at adjusted indent={}, no fallback needed\", adjusted_target);\n                }\n                // Scope exists at adjusted level, no need for fallback\n            } else {\n                // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at indent={} or adjusted_indent={}, creating fallback scope at target_indent\", target_indent, adjusted_target);\n                }\n                let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n                self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n            }\n        }\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n    ///\n    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to current scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    ///\n    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n    ///\n    /// // Second occurrence fails\n    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n        // Check if key exists before adding\n        if self.contains_key(key) {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n                key: key.to_string(),\n                scope_path,\n                first_line: scope.start_line,\n                duplicate_line: line,\n            })\n        } else {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            scope.add_key(key);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n    ///\n    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A string representing the scope path\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n        let mut path = Vec::new();\n        for scope in &self.scopes {\n            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n                path.push(key.clone());\n            }\n        }\n        path.join(\".\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get current indent level\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n    ///\n    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n    }\n\n    /// Get the base indentation size\n    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.base_indent\n    }\n\n    /// Set the base indentation size\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n    /// ```\n    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\n                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n                line,\n                indent_level,\n                self.depth(),\n                self.get_scope_path()\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level, but preserve parent mapping scopes\n        // When entering a sequence scope, we need to keep the parent mapping scope intact\n        // while clearing any nested scopes from previous content\n        let before_depth = self.depth();\n        let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n                .filter(|s| s.indent_level >= indent_level && s.parent_key.is_none())\n                .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n                .collect();\n            if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removing {} non-parent scopes: {:?}\",\n                         scopes_to_remove.len(),\n                         scopes_to_remove);\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level || s.parent_key.is_some());\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            let removed_count = before_depth - self.depth();\n            if removed_count > 0 {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n                         removed_count,\n                         before_path,\n                         self.get_scope_path());\n            }\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level, preserved parent mappings\", indent_level);\n        }\n\n        // Check if there's already a scope at this level that's in a sequence context\n        let needs_new_scope = self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level != indent_level || !scope.in_sequence_context)\n            .unwrap_or(true);\n\n        if needs_new_scope {\n            // Create a new scope for this sequence item\n            let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n            new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n            self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n            new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n            }\n        } else {\n            // Reset the existing scope for a new sequence item\n            if let Some(scope) = self.scopes.last_mut() {\n                scope.keys.clear();\n                scope.start_line = line;\n                self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n                scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (reset), item_id={}, reset existing sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n///\n/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n/// in the current scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n    /// The duplicate key\n    pub key: String,\n    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n    pub scope_path: String,\n    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n    pub first_line: usize,\n    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n}\n\nimpl DuplicateKeyError {\n    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            key,\n            scope_path,\n            first_line,\n            duplicate_line,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a formatted error message\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A human-readable error message\n    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for DuplicateKeyError {}\n\n/// Key context classification\n///\n/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\npub enum KeyContext {\n    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n    ///\n    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n    InlineScalar {\n        /// The key name\n        key: String,\n        /// The scalar value\n        value: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n    ///\n    /// The key's value is a nested mapping (indentation increases after this key)\n    ParentMapping {\n        /// The parent key name\n        key: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n    ///\n    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n    ParentSequence {\n        /// The parent key name\n        key: String,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl KeyContext {\n    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The key name as a string slice\n    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n        match self {\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n                write!(f, \"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\", key, value)\n            }\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n                write!(f, \"ParentMapping(key='{}')\", key)\n            }\n            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extract key context from a line\n///\n/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n///\n/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n///\n/// # Returns\n///\n/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n///\n/// // Inline scalar\n/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n///\n/// // Parent mapping\n/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n/// ```\npub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n    // Find colon position\n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n\n    // Skip if key is empty\n    let key = key_part.trim();\n    if key.is_empty() {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n\n    Some(context)\n}\n\n/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n///\n/// # Arguments\n///\n/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n///\n/// # Returns\n///\n/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n///\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n/// ```\npub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n}\n\n// Comprehensive tests are in the separate tests.rs file\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests;\n","numLines":930,"startLine":1,"totalLines":930}}}

This test suite verifies that the parser correctly handles YAML structures where\n//! indentation changes but no new keys are present, such as blank lines with visual\n//! indentation, scalar continuations, and other edge cases.\n//!\n//! Acceptance Criteria:\n//! - Indent changes without keys are detected correctly\n//! - Scope stack remains consistent after such events\n//! - No false duplicate key errors from indent-only changes\n//! - Edge cases are covered by tests\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::parser::{Parser, BasicParser};\n\n#[test]\nfn test_blank_lines_no_indent_changes() {\n    // Blank lines without indentation changes should be transparent\n    let yaml = r#\"\nkey1: value1\n\nkey2: value2\n\n\nkey3: value3\n\"#;\n\n    let parser = BasicParser::new();\n    let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n\n assert_eq!(value[\"key2\"], \"value2\");\n    assert_eq!(value[\"key3\"], \"value3\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_blank_lines_with_indent_ignored() {\n    // Blank lines with visual indentation should be ignored\n    // They should not affect scope tracking\n    let yaml = r#\"\nkey1: value1\n  # This blank line has 2 spaces but should be ignored\n\nkey2: value2\n\"#;\n\n    let parser = assert_eq!(value[\"key1\"], \"value1\");\n    assert_eq!(value[\"key2\"], \"value2\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_nested_structure_with_blank_lines() {\n    // Blank lines in nested structures should work test_scope_consistency_after_blank_lines() {\n    // Verify scope stack consistency after blank lines with different indents\n    let yaml = r#\"\nroot:\n  level1:\n    level2a: value1\n\n    level2b:
