Scope representation data structures\n//!\n//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n//!\n//! # Overview\n//!\n//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! services:\n//!   web:\n//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n//!     port: 8080\n//!   database:\n//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n//!     port: 5432\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The scope representation system tracks:\n//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n//!\n//! # Architecture\n//!\n//! ## Scope\n//!\n//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n//! - Line number where this scope started\n//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n//!\n//! ## ScopeStack\n//!\n//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n//!\n//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n//!\n//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n//!\n//! // Add keys to current scope\n//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n//!\n//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n//! ```\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::warn as log_warn;\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::trace as log_trace;\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n///\n/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    pub indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n            in_sequence_context: false,\n            sequence_item_id: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.keys.contains(key)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.keys.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n        self.keys.clear();\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n        }\n        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n///\n/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n    /// History of indent transitions (whether or not they have keys)\n    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>,\n    /// The last recorded indent level (to detect transitions)\n    last_indent: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Create a new scope stack\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n            base_indent,\n            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n            indent_transitions: Vec::new(),\n            last_indent: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n        // Debug logging for scope entry\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n            log_debug!(\n                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n                scope_type,\n                line,\n                indent_level,\n                parent_info,\n                self.depth(),\n                self.get_scope_path()\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n\n            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n            let before_depth = self.depth();\n            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n                    .filter(|s| s.indent_level > indent_level)\n                    .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n                    .collect();\n                if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removing {} scopes: {:?}\",\n                             scopes_to_remove.len(),\n                             scopes_to_remove);\n                }\n            }\n\n            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\n                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\n                if removed_count > 0 {\n                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n                             removed_count,\n                             before_path,\n                             self.get_scope_path());\n                }\n            }\n\n            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n            }\n        } else {\n            // Create new scope\n            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\", indent_level);\n            }\n        }\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n    /// ```\n    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\n                \"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\",\n                target_indent,\n                self.depth(),\n                self.current_indent(),\n                self.get_scope_path()\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\n        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\n            }\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\n        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\n            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n            .count() == 0;\n\n        if would_be_empty {\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\");\n            }\n            // Keep at least the root scope\n            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n        let before_depth = self.depth();\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\n        }\n\n        // Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n        // Check if there's a scope at target_indent + base_indent (for parent key scopes)\n        let adjusted_target = target_indent + self.base_indent;\n        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n            // Check if there's a scope at the adjusted level (parent key scope)\n            if self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == adjusted_target) {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Found scope at adjusted indent={}, no fallback needed\", adjusted_target);\n                }\n                // Scope exists at adjusted level, no need for fallback\n            } else {\n                // This shouldn't happen in valid YAML, but handle gracefully\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at indent={} or adjusted_indent={}, creating fallback scope at target_indent\", target_indent, adjusted_target);\n                }\n                let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n                self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n            }\n        }\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n    ///\n    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to current scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    ///\n    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n    ///\n    /// // Second occurrence fails\n    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n        // Check if key exists before adding\n        if self.contains_key(key) {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n                key: key.to_string(),\n                scope_path,\n                first_line: scope.start_line,\n                duplicate_line: line,\n            })\n        } else {\n            let scope = self.current_scope();\n            scope.add_key(key);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n    ///\n    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A string representing the scope path\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n        let mut path = Vec::new();\n        for scope in &self.scopes {\n            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n                path.push(key.clone());\n            }\n        }\n        path.join(\".\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get current indent level\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scopes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n    ///\n    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n    }\n\n    /// Get the base indentation size\n    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.base_indent\n    }\n\n    /// Set the base indentation size\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n    /// ```\n    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\n                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n                line,\n                indent_level,\n                self.depth(),\n                self.get_scope_path()\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Remove all scopes deeper than this level, but preserve parent mapping scopes\n        // When entering a sequence scope, we need to keep the parent mapping scope intact\n        // while clearing any nested scopes from previous content\n        let before_depth = self.depth();\n        let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n                .filter(|s| s.indent_level >= indent_level && s.parent_key.is_none())\n                .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n                .collect();\n            if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removing {} non-parent scopes: {:?}\",\n                         scopes_to_remove.len(),\n                         scopes_to_remove);\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level || s.parent_key.is_some());\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            let removed_count = before_depth - self.depth();\n            if removed_count > 0 {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n                         removed_count,\n                         before_path,\n                         self.get_scope_path());\n            }\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level, preserved parent mappings\", indent_level);\n        }\n\n        // Check if there's already a scope at this level that's in a sequence context\n        let needs_new_scope = self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level != indent_level || !scope.in_sequence_context)\n            .unwrap_or(true);\n\n        if needs_new_scope {\n            // Create a new scope for this sequence item\n            let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n            new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n            self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n            new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n            }\n        } else {\n            // Reset the existing scope for a new sequence item\n            if let Some(scope) = self.scopes.last_mut() {\n                scope.keys.clear();\n                scope.start_line = line;\n                self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n                scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (reset), item_id={}, reset existing sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n        self.scopes.last()\n            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Record an indent transition\n    ///\n    /// This method tracks indentation level changes during parsing, whether or not\n    /// they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables detection of indent changes\n    /// on blank lines, comments, or other non-key lines.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content (for debugging)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.record_indent_transition(5, 2, false, \"  # blank line with indent\");\n    /// ```\n    pub fn record_indent_transition(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) {\n        // Only record if the indent actually changed\n        if new_indent != self.last_indent {\n            let transition = IndentTransition::new(\n                line_number,\n                self.last_indent,\n                new_indent,\n                has_key,\n                raw_line,\n            );\n            self.indent_transitions.push(transition);\n            self.last_indent = new_indent;\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                let direction = if new_indent > self.last_indent.saturating_sub(1) {\n                    \"increase\"\n                } else {\n                    \"decrease\"\n                };\n                let key_status = if has_key { \"with-key\" } else { \"without-key\" };\n                log_debug!(\n                    \"[INDENT TRANSITION] line={}, {}→{}, {}, raw='{}'\",\n                    line_number,\n                    self.last_indent.saturating_sub(1),\n                    new_indent,\n                    key_status,\n                    raw_line.trim()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get all recorded indent transitions\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A slice of all indent transitions recorded during parsing\n    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&self) -> &[IndentTransition] {\n        &self.indent_transitions\n    }\n\n    /// Get indent transitions without keys\n    ///\n    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines without key tokens,\n    /// such as blank lines or comments.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred without keys\n    pub fn get_transitions_without_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n        self.indent_transitions\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|t| t.is_without_key())\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Get indent transitions with keys\n    ///\n    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines with key tokens.\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred with keys\n    pub fn get_transitions_with_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n        self.indent_transitions\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|t| t.has_key)\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all indent transitions\n    ///\n    /// This is typically called when resetting the scope stack for a new document.\n    pub fn clear_indent_transitions(&mut self) {\n        self.indent_transitions.clear();\n        self.last_indent = 0;\n    }\n\n    /// Get the last recorded indent level\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The most recent indent level recorded\n    pub fn get_last_indent(&self) -> usize {\n        self.last_indent\n    }\n\n    /// Set the last indent level directly\n    ///\n    /// This can be used to initialize the indent tracking or to correct it after\n    /// document markers or other special constructs.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent` - The indent level to set\n    pub fn set_last_indent(&mut self, indent: usize) {\n        self.last_indent = indent;\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n///\n/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n/// in the current scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n    /// The duplicate key\n    pub key: String,\n    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n    pub scope_path: String,\n    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n    pub first_line: usize,\n    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n}\n\nimpl DuplicateKeyError {\n    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            key,\n            scope_path,\n            first_line,\n            duplicate_line,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a formatted error message\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// A human-readable error message\n    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DuplicateKeyError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope '{}'\\n  First defined at: Line {}\",\n            self.duplicate_line, self.key, self.scope_path, self.first_line\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for DuplicateKeyError {}\n\n/// Key context classification\n///\n/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\npub enum KeyContext {\n    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n    ///\n    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n    InlineScalar {\n        /// The key name\n        key: String,\n        /// The scalar value\n        value: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n    ///\n    /// The key's value is a nested mapping (indentation increases after this key)\n    ParentMapping {\n        /// The parent key name\n        key: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n    ///\n    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n    ParentSequence {\n        /// The parent key name\n        key: String,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl KeyContext {\n    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// The key name as a string slice\n    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n        match self {\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n                write!(f, \"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\", key, value)\n            }\n            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n                write!(f, \"ParentMapping(key='{}')\", key)\n            }\n            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\n                write!(f, \"ParentSequence(key='{}')\", key)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extract key context from a line\n///\n/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\n/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n///\n/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\n///\n/// # Returns\n///\n/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\n///\n/// // Inline scalar\n/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n///\n/// // Parent mapping\n/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\n/// ```\npub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n    // Find colon position\n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n\n    // Skip if key is empty\n    let key = key_part.trim();\n    if key.is_empty() {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n\n    Some(context)\n}\n\n/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n///\n/// # Arguments\n///\n/// * `line` - The line to analyze\n///\n/// # Returns\n///\n/// The number of leading whitespace characters\n///\n/// # Examples\n///\n/// ```\n/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\n///\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"key: value\"), 0);\n/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    nested: value\"), 4);\n/// ```\npub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\n    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\n}\n\n/// Indent transition event recorded during parsing\n///\n/// This struct captures information about indentation level changes that occur\n/// during YAML parsing, whether or not a key token is present.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct IndentTransition {\n    /// The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line_number: usize,\n    /// The previous indentation level\n    pub from_indent: usize,\n    /// The new indentation level\n    pub to_indent: usize,\n    /// Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n    pub has_key: bool,\n    /// The raw line content (for debugging)\n    pub raw_line: String,\n}\n\nimpl IndentTransition {\n    /// Create a new indent transition record\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n    /// * `from_indent` - The previous indentation level\n    /// * `to_indent` - The new indentation level\n    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content\n    pub fn new(line_number: usize, from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            line_number,\n            from_indent,\n            to_indent,\n            has_key,\n            raw_line: raw_line.to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an indent increase\n    pub fn is_increase(&self) -> bool {\n        self.to_indent > self.from_indent\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is an indent decrease\n    pub fn is_decrease(&self) -> bool {\n        self.to_indent < self.from_indent\n    }\n\n    /// Get the indent change amount (positive for increase, negative for decrease)\n    pub fn change_amount(&self) -> isize {\n        self.to_indent as isize - self.from_indent as isize\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this transition occurred without a key\n    pub fn is_without_key(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.has_key\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for IndentTransition {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        let direction = if self.is_increase() {\n            \"increase\"\n        } else if self.is_decrease() {\n            \"decrease\"\n        } else {\n            \"no-change\"\n        };\n        let key_status = if self.has_key { \"with-key\" } else { \"without-key\" };\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"IndentTransition(line={}, {}→{}, {}, {})\",\n            self.line_number, self.from_indent, self.to_indent, direction, key_status\n        )\n    }\n}\n\n// Comprehensive tests are in the separate tests.rs file\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            // However, we DO track the indent transitions themselves for analysis.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Track indent changes even on blank lines for detection purposes\n                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, false, line);\n                }\n                continue;\n            }","originalFile":"//! Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the Parser trait and provides functionality for\n//! parsing YAML content from various sources.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::{\n    types::{ParseResult, ValidationResult, ValidationError},\n    error::ParseError,\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n    scope::ScopeStack,\n    line_parser::{calculate_indentation},\n    scope::extract_key_context,\n};\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n    ///\n    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Track blank lines with different indentation for debugging, but don't\n                // change scope. Scope transitions only happen on content lines.\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                if indent != scope_stack.current_indent() && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Blank line with indent change detected: line={}, indent={}, current_indent={}, skipping (will handle on next content line)\",\n                        line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.current_indent());\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        duplicate_errors\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for BasicParser {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Parser for BasicParser {\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Track blank lines with different indentation for debugging, but don't\n                // change scope. Scope transitions only happen on content lines.\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                if indent != scope_stack.current_indent() && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Blank line with indent change detected: line={}, indent={}, current_indent={}, skipping (will handle on next content line)\",\n                        line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.current_indent());\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n            Ok(value) => {\n                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n                ParseResult::success(value)\n            }\n            Err(err) => {\n                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n                    err.to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n                };\n                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Read file content\n        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n        } else {\n            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n        };\n\n        // Run syntax validation\n        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n\n        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n        if result.is_valid() {\n            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n\n            // Merge errors from detector\n            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Read file content\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => content,\n            Err(err) => {\n                return ValidationResult {\n                    valid: false,\n                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n                        path.display().to_string(),\n                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n                    )],\n                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n                };\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Validate the content\n        self.validate_str(&content)\n    }\n\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n        &self.config\n    }\n\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized,\n    {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod integration_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Test parse_str with nested YAML structures\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse valid nested YAML successfully\");\n\n        // Verify we can access the parsed structure\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with deeply nested YAML\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_deeply_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\napplication:\n  server:\n    config:\n      timeouts:\n        connect: 30\n        read: 60\n    logging:\n      level: info\n  database:\n    connection:\n      pool:\n        max: 10\n        min: 2\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse deeply nested YAML successfully\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that same key in different scopes passes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_same_key_different_scopes_passes() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        // This YAML has 'host' and 'port' in different scopes\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n  cache:\n    host: redis.example.com\n    port: 6379\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Same key in different scopes should be allowed\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let services = &value[\"services\"];\n        assert_eq!(services[\"web\"][\"host\"], \"localhost\");\n        assert_eq!(services[\"database\"][\"host\"], \"db.example.com\");\n        assert_eq!(services[\"cache\"][\"host\"], \"redis.example.com\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that duplicate key in same scope is detected by strict parser\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_same_scope_detected() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict(); // Strict parser doesn't allow duplicates\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nconfig:\n  server:\n    host: localhost\n    host: duplicate-value\n    port: 8080\n\"#;\n\n        // The parse_str itself may succeed (serde_yaml's behavior), but validation should detect the duplicate\n        let validation_result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!validation_result.is_valid(), \"Validation should detect duplicate keys\");\n\n        // Check that error message mentions duplicate key\n        let error_messages: Vec<String> = validation_result.errors\n            .iter()\n            .map(|e| e.message.clone())\n            .collect();\n        assert!(error_messages.iter().any(|msg| msg.contains(\"duplicate\") || msg.contains(\"host\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with sequence items having same keys\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_sequence_items_same_keys() {\n        let parser =      assert_eq!(value[\"parent\"][\"nested\"][\"deep\"], \"value3\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test sequence scope isolation\n    #[test]\n    fn test_sequence_scope_isolation() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  - id: 1\n    name: First\n    config:\n      enabled: true\n  - id: 2\n    name: Second\n    config:\n      enabled: false\n  - id: 3\n line)\n    #[test]\n    fn test_indent_transition_blank_line_only() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nlevel1:\n  level2:\n    key1: value1\n\n\nkey3: value3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Indent transition via blank lines should parse successfully\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert!(value.get(\"level1\").is_some());\n        assert!(value.get(\"key3\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test scope consistency after indent changes on blank lines\n    #[test]\n    fn test_scope_consistency_after_blank_indents() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nouter1:\n  inner1:\n    deep1: value1\n\n  inner2:\n    deep2: value2\n\nouter2:\n  inner3:\n    deep3: value3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Scope should remain consistent after indent changes on blank lines\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let outer1 = &value[\"outer1\"];\n        assert!(outer1.get(\"inner1\").is_some());\n value4\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_valid(), \"Blank lines should not cause false duplicate key errors\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test complex nesting
